observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory of PPEs,
label all properties with property tags/stickers and update the RPCPPE with full
and complete details to be submitted to the Audit Team not later than January 31
of the following year pursuant to Section 124 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
1.b. Require the Municipal Accountant and GSO to prepare, maintain, update and
complete the property ledger cards (PPELCs and RPLCs), and property cards,
respectively, for the properties of the Municipality in accordance with Sections 119
and 120 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
1.c. Require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the semi-expendable properties
still recorded in the PPE accounts and implement the new capitalization threshold
per Sections 4.1 and 4.2 of COA Circular No. 2022-004 in recording similar assets;
and
1.d. Prioritize the conduct of one-time cleansing of PPE balances as prescribed by
COA Circular No. 2020-006 taking strict consideration of the guidelines and
procedures on the preliminary activities, inventory taking, reconciliation, and
disposition and derecognition of non-existing/missing items for a valid reporting
and presentation of PPE accounts balances that are accurate and verifiable as to
existence, completeness and condition, thus attain fairness of presentation of all
PPE accounts in the Financial Statements of the Municipality at every reporting
period;

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to comply
strictly with the proper setting up of RPT/SET Receivables accounts in the books of
GF and SEF only, and make appropriate adjusting entries in the books of GF and TF to
correct improper recording of RPT Receivable accounts, including that of the affected
deferred accounts, pursuant to Sections 20 and 36 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs,
Volume I, so as to reflect reliable and accurate account balances and ensure fairness
of presentation in the financial statements of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to strictly comply with the
provisions of IPSAS 5 in capitalizing the borrowing costs to the cost of qualifying
assets.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:

a. compute for the withholding taxes on the payables amounting to P12,393,646.86
and make the necessary adjusting entries in the books. Henceforth, recognize in
the books the taxes withheld simultaneously with the setting up of Accounts
Payable for goods received or services rendered and inspected and accepted
pursuant to BIR MC No. 10-2018; and
b. make the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the noted
misclassification of accounts and ensure that any succeeding transactions of the
same kind are recorded in their corresponding accounts appropriately.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with the DSWD FO No. IV-A to address the discrepancies in the Due to
NGAs account, and make the necessary adjustment/s to ensure that the balance of
the account is reconciled with the books of the Source Agency.

We recommended and the Municipal Agriculturist agreed to trace the records of these
assets, regularly monitor the changes of Work/Zoo Animals and Breeding Stocks and
assist the Municipal General Services Officer in the conduct of the physical count
related to these living animals, including submission of an updated monitoring report
to the Municipal Accountant in order to reflect the accurate balances of these assets
in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Cause the possible designation of an additional Disbursing Officer, if warranted,
to prevent granting of various cash advances to the same Disbursing Officer; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure observance
of the maximum cash accountability of the Disbursing Officer in the granting of
cash advances and facilitate, if necessary, the increase in the amount of bond.

We recommended and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer agreed to
prepare and submit the Annual Work Program to the GenCO on or before March 15 of
every year, indicating therein the target beneficiaries and implementation date of
projects, duly supported by SB Resolution in accordance with Section 3.1 and 10 of
DOE DC No. 2018-08-0021 and Section 2.3 of the signed MOA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal General Services
and Engineering Offices to:

a. Submit copy of Purchase Orders and other forms of contract, together with its
supporting documents, to COA within five working days after their perfection
and/or approval as provided under COA Circular No. 2009-001, while Request for
Inspection and notice of delivery as well as the Acceptance and Inspection
Reports, be submitted to COA within 24 hours from receipt/acceptance of
goods/items/infrastructure projects, for the timely review and inspection; and
b. Strictly comply with the required information in every Purchase Orders and
Acceptance and Inspection Reports issued, pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-010
for clarity in the monitoring of goods delivered and accepted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Local Finance Committee to monitor the receipt of Share from IRA and ensure that
the appropriation for the 20 percent DF be at least 20% of the annual IRA
received by the Municipality, pursuant to Section 287 of RA No. 7160;
b. MDC to assess the viability of the identified Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs)
in the planning stage by conducting proper assessment and evaluation of the
priority projects to prevent or at least minimize series of changes of the plan to
ensure that only those projects, which are procurement-and-implementation-
ready would be given budget allocation;
c. Municipal Engineering Office, in coordination with the barangays and other
concerned officials, to perform in-depth planning and detailed engineering,
including thorough site inspections to anticipate and consider interruptions that
may probably arise during implementation of the projects to ensure the optimal
and timely implementation of the projects to be funded by the 20 percent DF;
d. MDC to submit the amended/supplemental Annual Investment Plan to the
Sangguniang Bayan and henceforth, secure their approval before any realignment
and disbursements of the 20% DF; and
Page 28 of 34
e. Municipal Budget Office, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and
Development Office, to trace the detailed PPAs under the Continuing
Appropriations to determine the unexpended balances of the completed projects
for reversion to unappropriated surplus for possible allocation to other
developmental projects necessary for the well-being of its constituents.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the
MDRRMO, in coordination with the MDRRMC, to:

a. Maximize the utilization of the LDRRM Funds and to accelerate their
implementation so that the objectives of the funds would be fully achieved and the
beneficiaries are protected against natural calamities and emergencies; and
b. Review the necessity and reasonable funding of each Program/Project/Activity
under the Capital Outlay and STF-Fund from Other Sources, in compliance with
Section 21 of RA No. 10121 and Item 5.1.12 and 5.1.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-
002, to ensure that these PPAs would be implemented barring any restrictions or
prohibitions, thus responsive to the objectives of strengthening the capacity to build
Page 31 of 34
disaster-resilient communities and in enhancing disaster preparedness at all levels.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Require the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) Executive Committee (Execom) to
submit the Annual GPB to the PPDO, from PPDO to DILG Provincial Office and
secure the Certificate of Endorsement from the DILG Provincial Office, where such
certification would truly establish the relevance of the gender issues identified
and gender-responsiveness of the PPAs indicated in the GPB pursuant to Section
5.0 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01; and
b. Require the GFPS Execom and Technical Working Group (TWG) to formulate GAD
Plan and Budget based on an updated GAD Database, to strengthen the planning
and budgeting processes for GAD, and ensure that planned PPAs would address
the identified gender issues and that objectives, performance indicators and
targets are well-formulated to facilitate monitoring and evaluation for the
attainment of GAD objectives.

observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant will prepare and adjusting entry for the carry-over balances of the Real Property Tax and Real Property Tax Receivables without supporting details to come up with reconciled balances starting CY 2024.

The Due from Other Funds of General Fund of P10,023,586.33 included the set-up receivables from Steel Corporation of the Philippines for the unpaid Business Taxes. The Receivables – Disallowances/Charges under General Fund included the disallowed Performance Based Bonus of P5,691,623.27. While the Due from Other Funds of Trust Fund included the amount for the transfer of the unexpended balance of the LDRRM Fund for CY 2023 totaling P8,552,173.77.
observations.reason: Substantial portion of unreconciled carried over balances in the Accounting records; adjustments to be made in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Strategically plan the application of one-time cleansing of PPE by setting
a deadline for each procedure on which the concerned units/personnel
shall follow;

b. Instruct the Heads of Accounting and Property Units to complete the
reconciliation of inventory count per RPCPPE with property and
accounting records, in compliance with Section 6.3 of COA Circular
No. 2020-006;

c. Complete the remaining procedures in application of one-time cleansing
of PPE as soon as possible; and

d. Instruct the Inventory Committee to prepare the RPCPPE in conformity
with the format and contents prescribed in Annex 49, Chapter II of the
Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II and completely fill up and include the
vital information on the report pertaining the actual result of the physical
inventory count undertaken.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Finance Committee to:

a) Analyze the trend in the collection of tax revenues for the last five years and
determine the amount of income which has reasonable assurance of
collectivity and use the data as inputs or benchmark in the preparation of
Annual Budget for the ensuing fiscal year; and

b) Prepare the Municipality’s plan to eliminate the cash deficit as soon as
possible and submit the same to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Evaluate the carried over beginning balances and coordinate with the
concerned agencies for the reconciliation thereof and remittance, if
possible;

b. Reconcile employees’ records with GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG to
identify the affected employees, if any, on the unremitted balances and
immediately remit the amounts due to these agencies and identify the
cause/s of the over remittance for some accounts and make the proper
adjustments to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary financial expenses in
the form of imposed penalties; and

c. Review the taxes withheld from employees, suppliers and contractors and
its corresponding remittances, and ensure that these taxes are remitted
within the prescribed period to avoid penalties and surcharges.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

a. Require the Municipal GSO to identify insurable properties of the
Municipality taking into consideration the current condition or
serviceability of each property, those which are not serviceable must not
be included and include in their Annual Investment Plan the cost of
premiums required for the insurance of these properties;

b. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds in the annual
budget subject to its financial capacity for the billed costs of insurance
premiums for all insurable property of the Municipality; and

c. Ensure that properties to be acquired by the Municipality in the future will
be fully and adequately insured in compliance with RA No. 656.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the Bids and Awards Committee
to:

a. Submit explanation/justification on the noted price difference of the procured
machinery and equipment to be used in the Materials Recovery Facility. Coordinate
with the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer for the justification on
the apparent non-utilization of the said assets; and

b. Submit an explanation why the delivered sound system was not in accordance with
the technical specifications provided for in the Purchase Order for further evaluation
by the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to
prepare and submit the required documents which must be duly authenticated as to
original copies.

Likewise, submit detailed justification to support that the communication wiring
totaling P1,058,862.28 under the mechanical works components was a necessary
and vital part of the project to prevent disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to
submit/provide:

a. Justifications and explanations regarding the overestimated Contract Cost
and the deficiencies due to the unaccomplished and some items of works
not in accordance with the specifications stipulated in the contract for
further evaluation by the Audit Team. Require the contractors to install the
unaccomplished works and rectify, if possible, the noted deficient items; and

b. Backup computation of the projects’ Approved Budget for the Contract for re-
evaluation to justify the excessive costs. Otherwise, the excess amount shall be
disallowed in audit. Henceforth, ensure that the quantities are reasonably
estimated to avoid excessive expenditures.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LDRRM Officer to:

a) Submit the Municipality’s plan for the implementation of unutilized balance
of LDRRM Fund amounting to P19,647,461.05;

b) Submit a written justifications and explanations regarding the above
observation on unutilized fund; and

c) Judiciously plan on how to maximize the utilization of the LDRRM Fund
and to accelerate their implementation in order to achieve the goal/target
of the Municipality regarding disaster relief, recovery, and risk reduction
and management.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor directs the:

a. Local Development Council (LDC) to assess the viability of the identified
PPAs in the planning stage to ensure that only those projects which are
procurement and implementation ready will be given budget allocation;

b. Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) to perform in-depth planning and
detailed engineering including thorough site inspections to anticipate and
consider interruptions that may probably arise during implementation of the
projects to ensure the optimal and timely implementation of the projects to
be funded by the 20% DF;

c. Municipal Budget Office in coordination with the Municipal Planning and
Development Office (MPDO) to trace the detailed PPAs under the Continuing
Appropriations to determine the unexpended balances of the completed
projects for reversion to unappropriated surplus for potential appropriation; and

d. LDC, MEO and MPDO to immediately look into the unimplemented projects
under the 20% DF and take appropriate actions on the causes noted of their
non-implementation. Formulate/develop specific strategies/ solutions to
Page 35 of 34

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a) Evaluate the necessity of appropriating Confidential Funds to the Annual
Budget considering the continual non-utilization and/or minimal utilization
of such for an extended period of time; and

b) If found necessary, assign a Project Accountable Officer to focus on the
preparation of the Physical and Financial Plan indicating the proposed
amount allocated for each program to be submitted to the Municipal
Budget Officer as support to the allocations of Confidential Funds
pursuant to Section 4.2 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular
No. 2015-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the other members
of the GFPS, to:

a. Modify the structure and composition of the GFPS to conform with the
prescribed organizational structure under Section 4.1(2) of PCW-DILG-
DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01;

b. Prepare GAD Agenda as soon as possible and strictly comply with the
provisions on the preparation of the GAD Agenda to ensure that the PPAs
indicated in the annual GPB are aligned with it or with the identified gender
issues to be addressed during the three-year term of the LCE’s leadership;

c. Submit the Annual GPB to the PPDO, from PPDO to DILG Provincial Office
and secure the Certificate of Endorsement from the DILG Provincial Office
before incorporating to the Municipality’s AIP and Annual Budget to
properly ascertain the relevance of the gender issues identified and
gender-responsiveness of the PPAs indicated in the GPB;

d. Exert extra efforts in accomplishing the targeted and planned Client and
Organization-Focused PPAs; and

e. Submit the GPB and GAD AR to the Audit Team within the prescribed
period to facilitate evaluation and validation of the GAD PPAs.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Coordinate with the Concerned Municipal GSO to evaluate the causes of the material discrepancy of P8,273,545.53 and make reconciliation accordingly. 
b. Instruct the Inventory Committee for one-time cleansing of PPE in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006. 
c. Complete the remaining procedures in application of one-time cleansing of PPE as soon as possible. 
d. Adopt the disposition procedures on the non-existing and/or missing PPEs as prescribed by Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction:
Not Material difference still existed in CY 2023 records, hence, reiterated in Observation No. 1, Part II, pages 46-50 of this Report.

Not Full application of one-time cleansing will be made in CY 2024.

PPE was already implemented. Preliminary activities were conducted in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006. Adopt the disposition procedures on the non-existing and/or missing PPEs as prescribed by Section 7 of the same Circular.
observations.reason: Not Material difference still existed in CY 2023 records; preliminary activities were conducted and one-time cleansing to be completed in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Prioritize the reconciliation and evaluation of available records and secure additional records to establish the updated status of the balances of fund transfers particularly those grants with carry over beginning balances; 
b. Coordinate with the implementing units to assess the needs for those unexpended fund balances from the NGAs. Should the funds be proven no longer needed, remit immediately to the SA, but if still needed, request authority to use the fund for other related projects of the Municipality; and 
c. Prepare the appropriate adjusting journal entries for the transfer of the grants and donations recognized in the GF book and submit to the Audit Team the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher (JEV).
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant will prepare and adjusting entry for the carry-over balances; available records and secure additional records.

The Municipal Accountant has made appropriate reconciliation of available records and secure additional records.

The GF, SEF and TF records were reconciled and the Municipality made appropriate adjustments.
observations.reason: Carry-over balances require evaluation and coordination with source agencies; some amounts were remitted or reclassified while others require authority or additional supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all future contracts for infrastructure projects to require the winning contractors of on-going infrastructure projects to submit their DOLE-approved CSHP, in compliance with D O L E Department Order No. 13, s. 1998 and Section 37.2.3(f) of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.  We further recommended that the BAC require the contractors to include in their program of works the cost of protective equipment to be provided to their laborers.
observations.managementAction:
The DOLE-approved CSHP of on-going infrastructure projects were already submitted. Majority of the projects were submitted.

The Municipal GSO has required winning contractors to submit DOLE-approved CSHP pursuant to the procurement and contract documents.

The Municipal Engineer submitted detailed justifications and explanations together with the required supporting documents in relation to the noted findings. Some of the observations were acknowledged by the agency and proposed alternative corrective actions.

The Municipal Engineer provided justifications and proposed rectification measures; Municipal Accountant closely monitored payments for infrastructure projects and supporting documents attached to disbursement vouchers prior making payment.
observations.reason: Submitted justifications and supporting documents; some observations acknowledged and corrective actions proposed; pending re-evaluation by COA Technical Audit Specialists.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: 
a. Municipal Engineer to submit justifications and explanations regarding the overestimated Contract Cost and noted delays in the implementation of the projects; 
b. Municipal Engineer to strictly monitor the completion of infrastructure projects as specified in the Contract Agreement and ensure that Time Extension Order are duly approved by the Local Chief Executive; and 
c. Municipal Accountant ensures that payments for infrastructure projects are supported with documents justifying delayed completion pursuant to 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, otherwise, liquidated damages be deducted from claims of contractors.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer submitted justifications and explanations together with the required supporting documents. The Municipal Engineer acknowledged discrepancies and advised contractors to rectify noted items to ensure compliance. Municipal Accountant closely monitored completion and supporting documents for disbursements.
observations.reason: Some observations acknowledged; corrective actions proposed; monitoring and rectification ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRM Officer to:
a. Submit the Municipality’s plan for the implementation of unutilized balance of LDRRM Fund amounting to P25,870,723.70; and 
b. Judiciously plan on how to maximize the utilization of the LDRRM Fund and to accelerate their implementation in order to achieve the goal/target of the Municipality regarding disaster relief, recovery, and risk reduction and management.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRM Officer submitted a revised and updated Annual Investment Plan on the target implementation of the P25,870,723.70. Management will make communication with the source agencies for the reprogramming of the financial assistance for Taal Volcano Eruption to other disaster-related PPAs.
observations.reason: The LDRRM Fund was still not optimally utilized in CY 2023; reprogramming and implementation pending coordination with source agencies.

observations.managementAction: Inventory reports together with the RIS and PTRs for all donations in-kind were already forwarded by the MHO to the Accounting Office. The donations in-kind with available cost information was recorded and consumed in CY 2023. The MHO will obtain necessary cost information for future donations and recognize receipts and issuances accordingly.
observations.reason: Donations in-kind were recorded where cost information was available; MHO to provide additional cost information for future donations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to employ the use of ATM payroll system for the payment of salaries to JO employees within three months from receipt thereof to deter possible risks of loss of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Majority of the JO employees have been issued an ATM for the payment of their salaries.

An Inventory Committee for one-time cleansing of PPE was already created. Preliminary activities were made. The Municipal GSO and Municipal Accountant made reconciliation and decreased the discrepancies on the PPE accounts accordingly. Investigation by the Inventory Committee is ongoing. Request for derecognition has not been made as the Inventory Committee is still in the preliminary stage of the one-time cleansing.
observations.reason: Inventory Committee created and preliminary activities underway; full one-time cleansing and derecognition pending; investigation in preliminary stage.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the pro-forma Notes to Financial Statements prescribed in Section No. 3.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-004 and ensure full compliance with disclosure requirements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reconciled the discrepancies noted in the financial statements and effected the necessary adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to submit the Bill of Quantities for the variation order in relation to the unaccomplished works amounting to P396,856.46 and demand payment of P717,119.62 from the Contractor for the cost of unaccomplished works; otherwise, concerned officials can be held personally liable for the said amount for failing to take necessary measures to ensure that the project is implemented in accordance with the approved plans and specifications.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer submitted the COA requested documents and explanations. Part of the noted deficiency amounting to P396,856.46 was submitted. The said deficiency has not been refunded and the proposed scope of works was forwarded to the COA TAS for further re-evaluation. No refund of the noted deficiency was made pending COA TAS re-evaluation.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents and explanations submitted; deficiency pending re-evaluation by COA TAS; refund not yet made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and members of the Municipal Development Council (MDC): a. Assess the feasibility of the implementation of the unimplemented projects and their responsiveness to the current needs of the constituents; and b. Ensure optimal and timely utilization of the 20% DF in the succeeding periods by including development projects that are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready in the local development plan, pursuant to DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1, s. 2020.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted status of noted PPAs under 20% DF as of May 15, 2024 showing several projects completed and others on-going; some projects unimplemented due to reasons such as adjacent property issues, site/location issues, lack of manpower in MEO for work program preparation. Management acknowledged discrepancy and submitted corrected Accomplishment Report.
observations.reason: Non-implementation due to site issues, lack of manpower, procurement timing; corrected reports submitted; some projects completed later.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the LDRRM Officer to: a. Incorporate in prior year’s planning for the utilization of the prior year’s unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in the LDRRMF Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) to maximize all available sources of DRRM Funds and include PPAs in the LDRRMFIP that can strengthen the capacity of the Municipality to build a disaster resilient community and enhance disaster preparedness and response and accelerate their implementation within the year as planned; and b. Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to evaluate and study the feasibility of the projects included in the LDRRMFIP particularly those held as continuing appropriations to ensure delivery of its intended services to the public.
observations.managementAction: Evaluation of the LDRRMFIP was conducted and projects to be included were identified. Management is amenable with the recommendations and committed to conduct comprehensive review of the fund’s utilization and implement corrective measures; will work closely with relevant departments to enhance transparency, accountability and efficiency in allocation and utilization of LDRRM Fund.
observations.reason: LDRRM Fund still not optimally utilized; evaluation and reprogramming in process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
● Prepare and maintain the Subsidiary Ledger and Local Road Network Ledger Card (LRNLC) for the road and its component namely: a) road lot, b) road pavement, c) drainage and slope protection structures and d) other miscellaneous structures; ● Prepare and submit the lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each road component at the end of the year in accordance with COA Circular 2015-008; and ● Disclose the total road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements. We also recommended that the GSO prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network and Report on the Local Road Network in the prescribed format; maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its component; Municipal Engineer to prepare the complete description and cost segregation; Municipal Accountant and GSO to secure from the Municipal Engineer the complete description and cost segregation of road components of every road project undertaken to have a basis in recording the road networks in the LRNLC and Local Road Network Property Card respectively.
observations.managementAction: On-going tracing of records by the Municipal Engineering Office; partial actions: Maintenance of reports and preparation of description and segregation being undertaken. Local Road Network Property Card will be prepared upon verification of the reports prepared by the Municipal Engineering Office. Once the cost component of the road networks is determined, proper recording will be undertaken by the Accounting Office and GSO.
observations.reason: Lack of complete information and segregation; on-going tracing and verification of records by MEO and GSO; disclosure pending completion of cost segregation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to recognize in the Municipality’s records and books of accounts the 23 parcels of land using the market value indicated in the earliest applicable Tax Declarations under the name of the Municipality; and to secure titles for the 18 parcels not yet covered by Certificate of Title and take necessary steps to protect the Municipality’s interests.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor started the retrieval and processing of titling of the land, because of the pending reconciliation of transfer of ownership and inconsistencies on the classification of the land. The substantial portion of the unreconciled differences which lacked available documents was being retrieved; process ongoing.
observations.reason: Pending reconciliation of transfer of ownership and classification inconsistencies; titling process ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. maintain individual subsidiary ledger card of taxpayers to ensure accuracy and reliability in the recognition of the RPT receivables; b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the list of delinquent real property tax as of year-end against the recorded RPT and SET Receivables to present an accurate and reliable receivable accounts in the financial statements; and c. convene the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer together with the Municipal Assessor to discuss thoroughly the matters on RPT and require these department heads to submit a commitment to the Municipal Mayor a timeline for the full implementation of the above recommendations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will prepare and adjusting entry for the carry-over balances of the RPT and SET Receivables without supporting details to have a corrected beginning balance of the accounts in the subsidiary ledgers. The concerned departments are continuously reconciling their records and retrieving any available documents. The Municipal Treasurer exhausted remedies for collection and legal measures where applicable.
observations.reason: Unreconciled differences due to carried-over balances; adjustments to be made in CY 2024; coordination and retrieval of documents ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local Finance Committee (LFC) to adopt careful and realistic planning and execution of the proposed annual budget on the basis of realistic projection of revenues for the ensuing years and conduct proper review of past collection performances; implement on-going intensive tax campaign and provision with complete logistics to attain the targeted revenue.
observations.managementAction: The Local Finance Committee’s analysis indicated the alleged under collection was due to uncollected receivables from Steel Corporation of the Philippines due to long-over-due legal dispute, non-attainment of projected market rental and projected collections from Business Permits for newly established businesses. The Revised Revenue Code for CY 2024 was implemented projected to increase Municipal revenue by 13.28%. As of April 30, 2024, cash position analysis showed ample cash to eliminate the cash deficit.
observations.reason: Under-collection due to specific uncollectible receivables and non-attainment of projected income; measures taken include revised revenue code and monitoring; full improvement to be assessed for CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the LFC to adopt strategy in order to increase revenue/collections and monitor the availability of cash every month to ensure that all income due the Municipality be collected in full and assure continuous flow of funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and LFC adopted strategies including revised revenue code for CY 2024 and continue to monitor cash inflows and outflows limiting expenditures within available collections. Revised revenue measures projected to increase revenue.
observations.reason: Cash deficit addressed through revised revenue measures and monitoring; full coverage of obligations to be achieved via improved collections and budget adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to determine the nature of the amount presented in Other Receivables for the amounts presented and reconcile GF, SEF and TF accounts; closely supervise personnel in charge in keeping the accounts for correctness and accuracy in the recording of transactions; and retrieve any available supporting documents and reconcile the balances accordingly.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reconciled the noted differences. The balances were addressed and reconciled. General ledger and subsidiary ledgers were reconciled accordingly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that concerned offices (MEO, BAC, Municipal Engineer) submit justifications and backup computations for re-evaluation, require contractors to install unaccomplished works and rectify deficient items and ensure quantities are reasonably estimated in future projects to avoid excessive expenditures.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer submitted detailed justifications and explanations together with supporting documents in relation to the noted findings. Some observations were acknowledged and proposed alternative corrective actions. Proposed rectification/adjustments programs were not yet forwarded to the Audit Team but justifications and additional documents were provided to COA TAS for re-evaluation.
observations.reason: Justifications submitted; rectification programs pending submission; further re-evaluation by COA TAS awaited.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the LDRRM Officer to submit implementation plan for unutilized balances, provide written justifications and judiciously plan to maximize utilization and accelerate implementation to achieve disaster relief, recovery and risk reduction goals.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to conduct comprehensive review of the fund’s utilization and implement corrective measures; will work closely with relevant departments to enhance transparency and efficiency. Submitted updated plans where applicable.
observations.reason: Unutilized balances remain; plan preparation and coordination ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Assessor to recognize the parcels in the books using market value per earliest tax declarations, secure titles, and remedy classification inconsistencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor started retrieval, processing of titling and reconciliation of ownership and classification; actions ongoing.
observations.reason: Titling and reconciliation in process; pending completion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledger cards and for Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to reconcile and submit timelines for full implementation of recommendations.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant will prepare adjusting entries for carry-over balances; departments are reconciling records and retrieving available documents. Some unreconciled differences to be adjusted in CY 2024.
observations.reason: Carried-over balances and documentation issues persist; adjustments scheduled for CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that LFC adopt more realistic revenue estimates, analyze collection trends, and implement revenue enhancement measures.
observations.managementAction: LFC conducted analysis citing specific uncollectible items and legal disputes; revised revenue code implemented for CY 2024 to increase revenue by 13.28%; monitoring ongoing.
observations.reason: Underlying uncollectible items and disputes; revenue measures implemented for CY 2024; monitoring continues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strategies to increase collections and monitor cash availability monthly.
observations.managementAction: Adopted strategies and revised revenue measures; monitoring of cash inflows and outflows ongoing.
observations.reason: Monitoring and revenue enhancement ongoing; full resolution depends on improved collections.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Enjoin the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer (GSO) to
identify the causes of the discrepancies between the PPE Ledger balances and
RPCPPE, and adjust their records accordingly. In addition, create a separate
Inventory Committee for one-time cleansing of PPE required under COA Circular
No. 2020-006 and approve the Physical Inventory Plan necessary to conduct the
physical inventory;
b. Instruct the duly created Inventory Committee, the Municipal General Services
Office and Municipal Accounting Office to adopt the general guidelines, procedural
guidelines, and reconciliation of inventory count per RPCPPE with property and
accounting records required under COA Circular No. 2020-006 in the conduct of
inventory taking for one-time cleansing of PPE;
c. Hire additional job order personnel, if necessary, to be included in the members of
the Inventory Committee or replace the members of the committee who will be
relieved of all their regular duties to ensure that the Inventory Committee will devote
their full time in the conduct of the physical inventory taking until the same is
completed and not to hamper the operations of the Municipality during inventory
taking;
d. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide budgetary allocation to cover the
necessary expenses in the conduct of inventory taking;
e. Cause the conduct of an investigation to determine the last known location/office
of non-existing/missing PPE items, the persons accountable therefor, the
circumstances of the loss, and the persons responsible for such loss. Issue
demand letters to the accountable personnel to produce the properties or pay the
depreciated replacement cost thereof, as provided in Section 7.0 of COA Circular
No. 2020-006;
f. Request authority from the COA for the derecognition in the books of accounts of
those non-existing/missing PPE items for which accountability could not be
established, in accordance with Section 8.0 of COA Circular No. 2020-006; and,
g. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the applicable accounting entries and
disclosure requirements in the Notes to the Financial Statements prescribed under
COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant regarding the
status and cost incurred for each project implemented by the Municipality,
particularly at year-end. As the Head of the Requesting Unit, he shall prepare the
Obligation Request and the Disbursement Voucher on a timely basis and forward
these, together with the appropriate supporting documents, to the Municipal
Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant, respectively; and,
b. Municipal Accountant to recognize the Accounts Payable and corresponding PPE
accounts for the unpaid balance of completed infrastructure projects as of
December 31 of each year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
observe the proper journal entries for recording of receipts and disbursements under
the Trust Fund. Refrain from making shortcut entries for proper monitoring of the
balances of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Observe the disclosure requirements in the preparation of the financial statements
in accordance with the IPSASs and COA Circular No. 2016-004 to assist users in
understanding the financial statements and to provide them with information
relevant to decision-making; and,
b. Reconcile the discrepancies noted in the financial statements and effect the
necessary adjustments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to prepare the list of unreleased checks every year-end in time
for the closing of the books and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant;
b. Municipal Accountant to restore to the Cash in Bank and proper liability accounts
the amount corresponding to the unreleased checks at year-end; and,
c. Municipal Accountant to adjust all valid reconciling items determined during the
preparation of the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements such as stale checks.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of all the
lacking documentary requirements to support the payment of the acquired land as
prescribed under RA No. 10752 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 for further
evaluation of the Audit Team; and,
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to consistently ensure the completeness of the
supporting documents before affixing her signature in the disbursement vouchers
and processing payments thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to immediately
submit the lacking documents noted above for further evaluation of the Audit Team,
and require the BAC to abide with the posting requirements; and,
b. Municipal Accountant to ensure that future disbursements must be supported with
complete required documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Accountable Officers to ensure that the grants of cash
advances are supported with the necessary documents, such as list of payees with
the amount of claims, payroll or schedule of the amount to be paid, among others,
to establish the validity of the claims;
b. Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of procuring goods and services from cash
advances, except for petty cash expenses in accordance with applicable laws, rules
and regulations;
c. BAC to submit justification on why no bidding was conducted and to strictly adhere
to the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 on the use of the
alternative mode of procurement, if competitive bidding is not viable;
d. Concerned Municipal personnel to cause submission of the lacking documents
supporting the liquidation reports for further evaluation of the Audit Team; and,
e. Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of documents supporting the
liquidation reports before recording the settlement of cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the Municipal Engineer to submit written justification on the noted
deficiencies in the implementation of the project;
b. Require full completion of the project or demand payment of P717,119.62 from the
Contractor for the cost of unaccomplished works amounting to P396,856.46 and
liquidated damages of P320,263.16; otherwise, concerned officials can be held
personally liable for the said amount for failing to take necessary measures to ensure
that the project is implemented in accordance with the approved plans and
specifications; and,
c. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to strictly monitor the implementation of projects in
accordance with the approved plans and specifications within the time frame
specified in the contract agreement. Impose regular validation of work
accomplishment and identification of contracts which require appropriate
management actions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. MENRO and Municipal Accountant to submit Accomplishment Reports/Service
Record for the hauling activities for the period April to December 15, 2021 for the
amounts billed by the Contractor for further evaluation of the Audit Team;
b. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and other concerned officials to take the
necessary procedures to correct or remedy the defects noted in the contract.
Include in future contracts all the required information listed in COA Circular No.
2012-001 and the pertinent Procurement regulations to protect the interests of the
Municipal government;
c. MENRO to adopt controls to monitor the Contractor’s compliance with the terms
and conditions of the contract, such as, but not limited to, formally appointing or
assigning competent employees to oversee and document the garbage collection
and validate or certify as to the correctness of the Billing Statements submitted by
the Contractor and indicate the actual volume of garbage hauled in the Inspection
and Acceptance Reports; and,
d. MENRO to require the assigned personnel to prepare a Record of
Attendance/Service of the Contractor indicating the date, driver’s full name, type of
vehicle, plate number, time in, time out, and signature of the driver and Certified
Correct by the MENRO to provide assurance of dates of disposal and hauling
services.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned Municipal officials to
prioritize the provision of a suitable and adequate size storage facility with installed
racks that can accommodate all the financial reports and other related documents of
the Municipality and its 48 barangays considering the ten-year preservation period of the
accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to record
all subsequent receipts of ER 1-94 Funds under the Share from National Wealth
account in the General Fund books of the Municipality, pursuant to IPSAS No. 1 and
BLGF Memorandum Circular No. 03-01-2017. Likewise, record the disbursements
therefrom in the appropriate asset and expense accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit justification why the
amount of P28,075,969.00 was disbursed even without the authority from the
Sangguniang Bayan rendering the transaction doubtful. Stop the practice of
disbursing the ER 1-94 Funds without approved ordinance and authority from the
Sangguniang Bayan;
b. Municipal Budget Officer to include the ER 1-94 Funds in the regular sources of
income for the budget year and carefully plan the expenditures/obligations that will
be charged therein, and ensure that subsequent disbursements are in accordance
with the approved appropriation ordinance; and,
c. Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to incorporate all available ER 1-
94 Funds in the Annual Work Program of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator and Municipal Accountant to strictly abide by the deadline
for the liquidation of ER 1-94 Funds set by the DOE, which is three (3) months from
utilization thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and members of the Municipal
Development Council:
a. Assess the feasibility of the implementation of the unimplemented projects and
their responsiveness to the current needs of the constituents; and,
b. Ensure optimal and timely utilization of 20 per cent Development Fund in the
succeeding periods by including development projects that are well-planned and
procurement-and-implementation-ready in the local development plan, pursuant to
DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1, s. 2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
ensure transfer of the unexpended balance of the 30 per cent Quick Response Fund
and 70 per cent Mitigation Fund – Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses of the
current year LDRRMF to the Trust Fund books under the Trust Liability- DRRM
account at the end of every year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. LDRRM Officer to incorporate in planning for the utilization of LDRRM Fund the
prior year’s unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in the LDRRMF Investment Plan
to maximize all available sources of DRRM Funds. Include PPAs in the LDRRMFIP
that can strengthen the capacity of the Municipality to build a disaster resilient
community and enhance disaster preparedness and response and accelerate their
implementation within the year as planned; and,
b. Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to evaluate and study
the feasibility of the projects, especially those held as continuing appropriations to
ensure delivery of its intended services to the public.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Provincial Accountant regarding the payment made by the Steel Corporation to effect the necessary adjustments and fully disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the actual status of said payment; reconcile records with the Provincial Accountant; furnish the Provincial Accountant with copies of the JEVs showing the amount due to LGU as the basis for recording the collections made by the Municipality in the books of the Province; and ascertain the nature of the recorded amount of ₱368,824.56 and if found invalid request the Commission on Audit for derecognition/adjustment of the dormant account balances in the books.
observations.managementAction: No adjustment was made pending the decision of the higher court regarding the case filed by the Government against Steel Corporation. No substantive action done due to pending legal matter. Municipal Accountant awaiting response from the Provincial Accountant and further documentation from the municipal office.
observations.reason: Pending litigation/court decision and lack of supporting documents from concerned parties prevented reconciliation and derecognition.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality refrain from disbursing municipal fund which is not in accordance with Section 335 of RA 7160 and require submission of justification/explanation for the difference noted in the number of rooms as well as submission of the attendance sheet for the 230 participants each day to validate the reliability and correctness of the cost of meals paid by the municipality.
observations.managementAction: No justification/explanation was submitted by the concerned municipal officials. Municipal management advised to refrain from future disbursements beyond allowable limits and to ensure compliance with RA 7160.
observations.reason: No written justification/explanation submitted by management; prior noncompliant disbursements remain unresolved.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the material deficiencies, we reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal
Mayor direct the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant:
a. to provide reconciliation of the differences noted and make appropriate
adjustments in their respective records, if necessary, in the course of
reconciliation of records made by the said offices in order to arrive at reliable
balances of PPE accounts;
b. strengthen the controls on the handling and recording procedures for the PPE by
performing a periodic reconciliation of the records of Accounting, the GSO and
actual inventory results;
c. exhaust efforts in the determining the details of the unaccounted balances of the
Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) from their oldest available records for
proper accountability and adjustment of accounts, otherwise, consider the
provisions of COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 prescribing the
guidelines and procedures on the disposition of non-existing/ missing PPEs
without available records of accountability;
d. investigate the causes of expired work/zoo animals and identify the
persons deemed liable thereof; and
e. Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books the unrecorded assets arising
from Trust Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to:
• prepare and maintain the Subsidiary Ledger and Local Road Network
Ledger Card for the road and its component namely: a) road lot, b) road
pavement, c) drainage and slope protection structures and d) other
miscellaneous structures;
• prepare and submit the lapsing schedule for the computation of the
depreciation for each road component at the end of the year in
accordance with COA Circular 2015-008; and
• disclose the total road network system in the Notes to Financial
Statements.
b. General Services Officer to:
• prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road
Network and Report on the Local Road Network in the prescribed
format; and
• maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads
and its component.
c. Municipal Engineer to prepare the complete description and cost
segregation of road components of every road project undertaken; and
d. Municipal Accountant and GSO to secure from the Municipal Engineer the
complete description and cost segregation of road components of every
road project undertaken to have a basis in recording the road networks in
the Local Road Network Ledger Card and Local Road Network Property
Card, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
record 15% mobilization fees and progress payments as Advances to Contractors and
Construction in Progress, respectively, and credit the same upon completion of the
projects using appropriate PPE accounts in order to arrive at reliable balances in the
Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Journal Entry Voucher
(JEV) to reclassify the erroneous entry of the ER 1-94 Funds to Share from National
Wealth in the General Fund instead of Due to NGAs in the Trust Fund to present the
correct year-end balance of the affected accounts.

We commended the Management of the Municipal Government of Balayan for the
efficient and prompt implementation of the identified programs, activities and projects
towards the achievement of BGCM’s objective.

However, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the subsidiary ledger for
the SAGF-BGCM in compliance with the Government Accounting Manual
(GAM) for LGUs for fair presentation, transparency and accountability of
the funds utilized;
b) the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries for the
receipt and utilization of the BGCM fund and use the appropriate accounts
in recording transactions in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009
and GAM for LGUs;
c) the Municipal Accountant and immediately present for audit the lacking
documents to substantiate aforementioned BGCM transactions and ensure
that all documentary requirements for emergency purchase are complied
with before certifying as to completeness of the supporting documents of
the disbursement vouchers; and
d) the Bids and Awards Committee to adhere to the provisions of IRR of RA
9184 and ensure posting of Notice of Award, Contract or Purchase Order
and Notice to Proceed if necessary, to the PhilGEPS website for future
procurement thru emergency cases.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
ensure transfer of the unexpended balance of the 30% QRF and 70% MF-MOOE of the
current year LDRRMF in the Trust Fund books under the Trust Liability-DRRM
account at year-end. Carry as continuing those capital outlay appropriations that are
unexpended/ unobligated or for projects that are not yet completed as of reporting
date.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the
Municipal Accountant to charge only eligible items against the fund following the COA
Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 on the allocation and
utilization of the 5% LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
record the transactions and utilization of Trust Liability- DRRM in the Trust Fund book
in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor requires the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC), Municipal General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to:
a. submit a valid explanation as to why the procurement of goods thru cash
advances failed to pass through the proper mode of procurement, and on the
inadequacy of documents supporting the disbursement vouchers;
b. BAC to strictly abide by the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184 on the use of the
alternative mode of procurement, if competitive bidding is not viable, and to stop
the practice of procuring goods from cash advances, except for petty cash
expenses covered by appropriate laws, rules and regulations;
c. MDRRMO, in the event of an emergency or state of calamity, to assess and
prepare the necessary supplemental plan, and thereafter request for the purchase
of the much needed goods/services upon approval of the HoPE and of the local
sangguniang, if warranted; and
d. Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of documents supporting the
disbursement vouchers before making any payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. the concerned Municipal Officials to immediately submit to the Audit Team
the copy of contract for the construction of new municipal hall/ government
center together with the required supporting documents for review and
evaluation. Strictly comply with the requirements on the submission of
contracts as provided in Commission on Audit Circular (COA) No. 2009-
001; and
b. the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to cause the
submission of the lacking documentary requirements for the progress
payments made by the Municipality to avoid suspension in audit.

We commended the Management for its compliance on the timely remittance of GSIS
contributions and loan amortizations. However, we recommended that the Municipal
Mayor require the:
a. Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) to ensure the sufficiency of the employee’s
net pay after deducting the loan amortizations prior to approving loans.
b. Municipal Accountant to deduct the correct amount of premiums and loan
amortizations due to avoid incurrence of penalties and remit the same
immediately to GSIS.
c. AAO and Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the GSIS for the
reconciliation of records to determine the correctness of the unpaid premiums
of PS and GS with a total amount of ₱510,823.95.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Social Welfare and
Development Officer to submit all the noted lacking documents of
beneficiaries such as accomplished Social Amelioration Card, photocopy
of the identification card with specimen signatures of the beneficiaries, and
photo of the beneficiaries during actual receipt of the assistance and
provide certification signed by the eleven (11) beneficiaries which had no
signature on the payroll that they actually received the cash assistance and
ensure the completeness of all the documentary requirements in the
subsequent programs similar in nature; and
b. Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to submit written
explanation on the deficiencies noted as a result of confirmation by the
Audit Team on dividing the ₱6,500.00 received for those who were not able
to fill up Social Amelioration Cards.

In view of the material deficiencies, we reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor: a. to provide reconciliation of the differences noted and make appropriate adjustments in their respective records, if necessary, in the course of reconciliation of records made by the said offices in order to arrive at reliable balances of PPE accounts; b. strengthen the controls on the handling and recording procedures for the PPE by performing a periodic reconciliation of the records of Accounting, the GSO and actual inventory results; c. exhaust efforts in the determining the details of the beginning balances of the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) from their oldest available records for proper accountability and adjustment of accounts; and d. Immediately comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the reporting of road networks.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant and GSO/Property Officer provide reconciled RPCPPE and submitted updated RPCPPE to Accounting Office.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2020, RPCPPE and reconciled their records with Accounting unit discrepancy amounting to ₱2,855,498.35. Reiterated on Audit Team thru email on June 15, 2020.

observations.recommendation: a. General Services Officer, the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to determine the nature and amount of acquisition of lots, as basis of valuation; b. General Services Officer conduct sites inspection and prepare the reports on the actual usage of each parcels of land and existence of unlawful occupants, if any, and provide the said report to the Municipal Accountant; c. Municipal Accountant to recognize in the Municipality’s books the twenty-three (23) parcels of land using the market value indicated in the earliest applicable Tax Declarations under the name of the Municipality; and d. Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Assessor include in the appropriations the titling of land owned by the Municipal Government.
observations.managementAction: Conducted sites inspection and prepared the reports together with all the documents such as sketch, pictures and submitted the same to the Municipal Accountant on July 20, 2020.
observations.reason: Documentations for the titling are still in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Municipal Treasurer to provide the certified list of taxpayers with their corresponding amount due and collectible for the year; b. Municipal Accountant to record collection of receivables based on gross amounts of taxes collected and set-up receivables based on the total collectibles for the year as provided by the Municipal Treasurer; c. Municipal Accountant to maintain individual subsidiary ledger card of taxpayers to ensure accuracy and reliability in the recognition of the RPT receivables; and d. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the list of delinquent real property tax as of year-end against the recorded RPT and SET Receivables to present an accurate and reliable receivable accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Treasurer has been providing the certified list of taxpayers with their corresponding amount due and collectible for the year thereafter; Starting CY 2020, collections were recorded based on gross amounts of tax collected. The Municipal Accountant maintains individual subsidiary ledger cards. On-going reconciliation of records between Treasurer and Accounting offices.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of records between Treasurer and Accounting Office is on-going. The Office of the Municipal Accountant is to maintain individual ledger card for taxpayers, their Office have available records of Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) for reconciliation with Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the reconciliation either on the face of the SCBAA or in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with paragraph 47 of PPSAS 24 to achieve transparency in financial reporting.
observations.managementAction: disclosed the reconciliation of SCBAA in the Notes to Financial Statements as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to initiate a thorough review of the affected accounts to determine the cause/s of under and over remittance and make proper adjustments/corrections relative thereto. Moreover, ensure that all accounts are in their normal balances and fairly presented in the resulting financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Due to GSIS discussed on Observation No. 11, page 64. Verification of subsidiary ledgers still reflected abnormal balances on some accounts.
observations.reason: Timing differences and technical differences with concerned agencies. Verification of subsidiary ledgers still shows abnormal balances on some accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: a. the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit to the Accounting Office the List of Municipal Unreleased Checks in time for the closing of the books to enable the reversion back to cash in bank and payable accounts of the amount of the unreleased checks and submit the Report of Checks Issued and submitted to the Office of the Municipal Accountant for adjustments in the financial statements; and b. the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments of reconciling items determined during the preparation of the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements on a timely basis for an accurate and reliable Cash in Bank account balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Some of the stale checks which have been included in the list are already claimed by the payee and reported in the Report of Checks Issued and submitted to the Office of the Municipal Accountant for accounting adjustments in the Municipality’s financial book of accounts.
observations.reason: Some of the stale checks remaining on the custody of the Municipal Treasurer were not reverted back to Cash in Bank account and payable accounts. Payees were neither reported in the unclaimed list nor presentation to bank for encashment thus resulting reconciling items remained unresolved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Undertake careful planning and execution of proposed annual budget on the basis of realistic projection of revenues for the ensuing year and conduct proper review of past collection performances; and b. Conduct periodic Local Finance Committee assessment to ascertain whether collection targets are attained. The reasons for the shortfall in collections should be analysed and alternative actions should be looked into to avoid incurrence of misses in projections in the future.
observations.managementAction: The Local Finance Committee conducted periodic review of past performances. The Municipal Mayor and concerned offices conducted project planning and revenue campaign. Intensive tax collection and provision with more effort on tax revenue collection was undertaken.
observations.reason: Intensive tax campaign and provision for logistics were done but target revenue/collections for CY 2020 were not fully attained. LFC will prepare request for reversal of continuing accounts without cash allocations addressed to SB.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: a. the concerned Municipal Officials to submit to the Audit Team the credentials as to the expertise, experience, and capabilities of the six consultants; and b. the Municipal Engineer to request submission of the lacking documentary requirements as provided in Section 9.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to consider the transaction allowable in audit.
observations.managementAction: The credentials and the contract of consultants were submitted to the Audit Team on August 24, 2020. The Municipal Engineer submitted the required documents to the audit team on August 7, 2020. Other supporting documents were not yet submitted for verification.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents were not yet submitted for verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the paid disbursement vouchers including supporting documents to the Audit Team for verification and post audit as required by COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: All disbursement vouchers were submitted to the Audit Team on January 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to fully comply with the aforementioned law, rules and regulations on the submission of the year-end financial statements and supporting schedules of the barangays and require these department heads to submit a commitment to the Municipal Mayor a timeline for the full implementation of the above recommendations in order to address the increasing uncollected real property taxes and to assure that the Municipality account an accurate and reliable RPT receivables.
observations.managementAction: 40 out of 48 barangay financial demand letters were submitted to the Audit Team. Despite repeated request, some barangay treasurers refused to deliver their documents on time.
observations.reason: Particularly of the 48 barangays, many refused to submit their year-end accounting and auditing documents on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer-Designate to meticulously and judiciously plan procurement for each quarter as consolidated and incorporate them in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) in order to adopt competitive public bidding to provide reasonable assurance that the best quality at the lowest price is availed. Ensure that only those that are emergency in nature and those ordinary or regular supplies and materials not available in the Procurement Services (PS) and within the threshold are purchased through alternative modes of procurement.
observations.managementAction: For the purchase of equipment, the municipality consolidated all requisitions and hired new young professionals for social services. IT equipment were upgraded and needs for logistic equipment were addressed with other departments.
observations.reason: There were still departments that had adhoc requirements; consolidation improved but some procurements remained through alternative methods due to exigency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the contractors and require them to refund the amount on the deficiency noted by the COA TAS and submit the required documents to the audit team.
observations.managementAction: Photocopies of supporting documents were submitted to the audit team on August 7, 2020. The Municipal Engineer also submitted a written explanation on the deficiency noted by TAS on April 29, 2021 for evaluation of the COA TAS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer and Municipal Engineer to submit copies of Contracts and purchase Orders and their supporting documents to the Audit Team within five working days upon approval in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001; the GSO designate Inspector to furnish the audit team copies of delivery documents as required under COA Circular No. 2009-002 to facilitate the timely conduct of inspection of the delivered items; and the Municipal Engineer to request to the audit team for the inspection of completed projects as required by existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Submitted contracts with supporting documents to the audit team starting CY 2021. The GSO designated an inspector and furnished the audit team copies of delivery documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: a. MDRRMO to prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF with the prescribed format provided in COA Circular 2012-001 and submit certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council; b. Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance for CY 2019 amounting to ₱8,156,416.23 in the Trust Fund books; c. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to effect necessary journal entries in the accounting books and Registry of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligation, respectively, for the reversion of LDRRMF amounting to ₱15,138,092.73 lodged in the trust fund books which remained unutilized for more than five years; d. Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to charge only eligible items against the fund and ensure charges to Quick Response Fund are supported with Sangguniang Bayan Resolution declaring state of calamity; and e. MDRRMO to submit the Sangguniang Bayan Resolution declaring state of calamity for purchases for Bagyong Tisoy to the audit team for verification.
observations.managementAction: MDRRMO prepared and regularly submitted the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to the audit team. Municipal Accountant transferred in the Trust Fund books only on March 2021. Administrative action to transfer continuing CO balances was initiated. Municipal Budget Officer indicated that charges to administrative expenses were shifted to General Fund starting CY 2021. MDRRMO explained purchases for stock pile/reserves.
observations.reason: Unexpended balances were transferred late (March 2021). Some continuing appropriations remained in General Fund as on-going projects. Administrative expenses charged to LDRRMF were already charged to General Fund starting CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person and Municipal Budget Officer to prepare GAD budget in accordance with JMC No. 2013-01 and to strive for the full implementation of GAD-related PPAs by maximizing the utilization of the amount set aside for said purpose so that the desired outcomes are attained and benefits enjoyed by the intended GAD beneficiaries; and GAD Focal Person to submit to the audit team a copy of the approved GPB to facilitate review of the same.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality still allocated 5.23% for GAD for CY 2020. Soft copy of the approved GPB was submitted thru email to the audit team on June 9, 2021. The MSWDO submitted GAD Plan and accomplishment report upon request of the audit team.
observations.reason: GAD Plan and GPB were submitted only upon request of the audit team; earlier non-submission remained an issue.

observations.recommendation: We required the Local Finance Committee to adopt strategy in order to increase revenue/collections and monitor local finance to ensure that all income due the Municipality be collected in full and assure continuous flow of funds; and b. Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to exercise diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of the Municipality’s funds and limit expenditures within the total income realized and collected during the year to avoid incurrence of cash overdraft/deficit.
observations.managementAction: The Local Finance Committee conducted meetings and agreed that the Municipality will conduct intensive tax collection and project planning. The Municipal Budget Officer reported limited resource of the Municipality due to COVID-19 which affected attainment of target revenues.
observations.reason: Despite measures, the Municipality did not achieve the target revenue/collections for CY 2020; limited resources due to pandemic affected attainment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer verify and evaluate the nature and authenticity of the above transactions and prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect adjustments for these checks to present an accurate and reliable balance of the Cash in Bank accounts.
observations.managementAction: Journal Entry Voucher No. 2019-05 dated May 31, 2019 was prepared amounting to ₱120,302.76 to adjust certain stale checks. Some of the payees claimed the checks after verbal and written notices were made. The remaining stale checks were included in a list and actions for encashment/ reissuance are ongoing.
observations.reason: Some stale checks remain unresolved and reconciling items persist as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjustments in the municipality’s financial statements to have a reconciled balance of reciprocal accounts; determine the nature of the amount presented in Other Receivables for the account classification of receivables from SEF/GF amounting to ₱49,348.60; and supervise personnel to properly keep records and effect classification corrections in GF/SEF and Trust Fund books.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were effected in April 2019 (JEV No. 2019-04-005 and JEV No. 2019-04-006) to recognize the ₱10 Million and reconcile other fund balances. Municipal Accountant to closely supervise bookkeepers and requested personnel to maintain proper accounts.
observations.reason: Some reciprocal entries were adjusted, but complete reconciliation across all funds remained on-going; documentation and final clearing of dormant accounts pending.
