observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) propose policy/guideline in the billing and collection of electricity consumption of various stallholders and the municipal market; b) Market Administrator to coordinate with BATELEC for separate billing of stallholders’ electric consumption and payments to be made directly to the concessionaire if feasible; and c) Accounting Office to require monthly list of stallholders with corresponding amount of electric consumption and the amount paid so that the former can separately record receivables pertaining to stallholders.
observations.managementAction: Market Administrator regularly provides list of stallholders with corresponding amount of electric consumption to Accounting Office. Negotiations with BATELEC ongoing for separate billing of stallholders. GSO submitted representation requesting separate billing.
observations.reason: Policy/guideline was made and operational; however BATELEC has not agreed to separate meter billing in all cases; matter being coordinated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify the erroneous entry of the Small Town Lottery (STL) share to Other Income-Share from PCSO instead of Trust Liability-Due to LGUs in the Trust Fund to present the correct year-end balance of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: Copy of Memorandum of Agreement between municipality and PCSO covering the PCSO shares was submitted to the audit team and the JEV reclassifying the entries was prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer and the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere with provisions of the GAA such that employees net take home pay is at least ₱5,000.00 before approving loans and that personnel processing loan applications ensure thorough review and computation compliance on threshold requirement on net take home pay amount.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Management issued Memorandum No. 2019-05-047 stating that employees with net take home pay below ₱5,000.00 shall no longer be granted loan starting June 1, 2019. The Office implemented strict verification in loan processing.
observations.reason: Some employees had loans granted before implementation; monitoring to ensure compliance ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor discourage frequent changes in the preparation of the annual budget or any practice that undermines the integration of planning and budgeting at the local level and ensure compliance with Section 321 of RA 7160 and Article 417 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations.
observations.managementAction: Realignments were made in view of COVID-19 in 2020 to utilize unused budget for emergency response and relief; the municipality implemented controls to minimize future frequent budget realignments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer, the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and other concerned officials to submit at the beginning of the year the lists of all ongoing government projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those to be implemented during the year, in compliance with the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer submitted lists of projects to be implemented at the beginning of 2021 and onward. Ongoing monitoring of project reporting established.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person and concerned officials and employees involved in the preparation of budget to see to it that the funding requirement for GAD is fully complied with and that the GAD Plan and Budget be submitted to COA to facilitate review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Government allocated 5.23% for GAD for CY 2020. Soft copy of the approved GPB and GAD Plan were submitted to the audit team on June 9, 2021.
observations.reason: GAD Plan and GPB were submitted only upon request of the audit team; earlier non-submission remained an issue.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a) coordinate with the Provincial Accountant regarding the payment made by the Steel Corporation of the Philippines to effect the necessary adjustments and to fully disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the actual status of said payment; b) reconcile their records with the Provincial Accountant and settle discrepancies between the balances of reciprocal accounts so that the Municipality will be able to present the correct balances of inter-agency receivables and payables in the financial statements; c) furnish the Provincial Accountant with the copies of the JEVs showing the amount due to LGU as basis for recording the collections made by the Municipality in the books of the Province; d) analyze fully the nature of each transaction and reclassify to appropriate accounts; and e) ascertain the nature of the recorded amount of ₱368,824.56 and if found that it is not valid request from the Commission on Audit be secured for the derecognition and/or adjustment of the dormant account balances in the books.
observations.managementAction: No adjustment has been made for the payment regarding Steel Corporation as still waiting for the decision of the higher court regarding the case filed by the provincial government. No action done by the municipal management regarding reconciliation with the Provincial Accountant. No action done by the municipal Accountant to furnish the Province with copies of the JEVs. No action done to ascertain the nature of ₱368,824.56.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the material discrepancy of the PPE accounts and continuous failure of the
Municipality to correct such deficiencies, we reiterated our recommendation that the
Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant and GSO/Property Officer provide reconciliation of the difference noted and make appropriate
adjustments in their respective records, if necessary, in the course of reconciliation of
records made by the said offices in order to arrive at reliable balances of PPE
accounts;
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the balances of the reported Property,
Plant and Equipment in view of the discrepancy of ₱28,766,115.24 between the
reported purchase and construction of PPE in the Cash Flow Statement and the total
additions to PPE in the Notes to Financial Statements in CY 2019;
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal General Services
Officer to determine the details of the beginning balances of the Property, Plant and
Equipment (PPE) from their oldest available records for proper accountability and
adjustment of accounts; and
d. Immediately comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines provided in COA
Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 to fairly present the LGU’s financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. General Services Officer, Municipal Assessor, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal
Accountant to determine the nature and amount of acquisition of these lots, as basis
of valuation;
b. General Services Officer and Municipal Assessor to conduct sites inspection and
prepare the reports on the actual usage of each parcels of land and existence of
unlawful occupants, if any, and provide the said report to the Municipal
Accountant;
c. Municipal Accountant to recognize in the Municipality’s books of accounts the
twenty-three (23) parcels of land using the market value indicated in the earliest
applicable Tax Declarations under the name of the Municipality; and
d. Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Assessor include in the appropriations
the titling of land owned by the Municipal Government.

Time and again, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant the certified list of
taxpayers with their corresponding amount due and collectible for the CY
2020 and years thereafter;
b. Municipal Accountant to record collection of receivables based on gross
amounts of taxes collected and set-up receivables based on the total
collectibles for the year as provided by the Municipal Treasurer;
c. Municipal Accountant to maintain individual subsidiary ledger card of
taxpayers to ensure accuracy and reliability in the recognition of the RPT
receivables; and
d. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the list of
delinquent real property tax as of year-end against the recorded RPT and
SET Receivables to present an accurate and reliable receivable accounts in
the financial statements.

Aside from these perennial audit recommendations, we recommended also that the
Municipal Mayor convene the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer
together with the Municipal Assessor to discuss thoroughly the matters on RPT and
require these department heads to submit a commitment to the Municipal Mayor a
timeline for the full implementation of the above recommendations in order to
address the increasing uncollected real property taxes and to assure that the
Municipality account an accurate and reliable RPT receivables.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
disclose the required reconciliation either on the face of the SCBAA or in the Notes to
Financial Statements in compliance with paragraph 47 of PPSAS 24 to achieve
transparency in financial reporting.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
initiate a thorough review of the affected accounts to determine the cause/s of under
and over remittance and make proper adjustments/corrections relative thereto.
Moreover, ensure that all accounts are in their normal balances and fairly presented
in the resulting financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. the Municipal Treasurer to a) prepare and submit to the Municipal
Accounting Office the List of Unreleased Checks in time for the closing of
the books to enable the reversion back to cash in bank and payable
accounts of the amount of unreleased checks in order to fairly present the
cash in bank and other affected accounts in the financial statements and b)
for the checks which have become stale, send written notice to the payees
for the existence of checks and require submission to facilitate issuance of
new checks; and
b. the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments of
reconciling items determined during the preparation of the Monthly Bank
Reconciliation Statements on a timely basis for an accurate and reliable
Cash in Bank account balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Finance Committee (LFC)
to:
a. Undertake careful planning and execution of the proposed annual budget on the
basis of realistic projection of revenues for the ensuing year and conduct proper
review of past collection performances.
b. Conduct periodic assessment to ascertain whether collection targets are
attained. The reasons for the shortfall in collections should be analysed and
alternative actions should be looked into to avoid incurrence of misses in
projections in the future.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. The concerned Municipal Officials to submit credentials as to the experience,
expertise and capabilities of consultants to support their selection; and
b. The Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to strictly comply with the
requirements on the procurement of consulting services and immediately submit the
lacking documentary requirements provided in Section 9.1.2 of COA Circular No.
2012-001 to consider the transaction allowable in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
submit immediately the paid disbursement vouchers including supporting documents
to the Audit Team for verification and post audit as required by COA Circular No.
2009-006 and Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant fully comply with the
aforementioned law, rules and regulations of government accounting and auditing on
the submission of the year-end financial statements particularly of the 48 barangays
of the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer-
Designate to meticulously and judiciously plan procurement for each quarter as
consolidated and incorporate them in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) in order to
adopt competitive public bidding to provide reasonable assurance that the best
quality at the lowest price is availed. Ensure that only those that are emergency in
nature and those ordinary or regular supplies and materials not available in the
Procurement Services (PS) and within the threshold are purchased through
alternative modes of procurement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to
coordinate with the contractors and require them to refund the amount of deficiency
noted by the COA TAS and submit the required documents to avoid
suspension/disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. General Services Officer and Municipal Engineer to submit copies of
Contracts and Purchase Orders and their supporting documents to the
Audit Team within five working days upon approval in compliance with
COA Circular No. 2009-001;
b. Designated Inspector to furnish the Audit Team copy of delivery
documents as required under COA Circular No. 2009-002 to facilitate
the timely conduct of inspection of the delivered items; and
c. Municipal Engineer to request to the audit team for the inspection of
completed projects as required by existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. MDRRMO to prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of
LDRRMF with the prescribed format provided in COA Circular 2012-001
certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and submit on or before the
15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local
Development Council;
b. Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF-
MOOE and QRF for CY 2019 amounting to ₱8,156,416.23 in the Trust Fund
books;
c. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to effect the necessary
journal entries in the accounting books and Registry of Appropriation,
Allotment and Obligations, respectively, for the reversion of LDRRMF
amounting to ₱15,138,092.73 lodged in the trust fund books which
remained unutilized for more than five years.
d. Direct the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to
charge only eligible items against the fund and ensure that charges to
Quick Response Fund are supported with Sangguniang Bayan Resolution
declaring state of calamity; and
e. MDRRMO to submit the Sangguniang Bayan Resolution declaring state of
calamity for Bagyong Tisoy to the audit team for verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person and
Municipal Budget Officer to prepare GAD budget in accordance with the
aforementioned JMC.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to
submit to the Audit Team copy of the approved GPB to facilitate review and evaluate the
plan and reported accomplishments.

observations.managementAction:
Conducted physical inventory of serviceable and unserviceable properties. Submitted the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment on February 14, 2020.
The Accounting Office furnished the GSO of the accounts that updated were not reconciled.
Unserviceable properties were provided with disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements. Request for inspection was returned by the Audit Team to the GSO for completion of request for documents. Also, last March 4, 2020. ₱6,322,142.00 of unserviceable properties were provided with request.
observations.reason: Prior year’s balance had no detail as to the description, date of acquisition and whereabouts of property.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a) Local Finance Committee to adopt strategy in order to increase revenue/collections and monitor local finance committee to ensure that all income due the Municipality be collected in full and properly monitor local finance committee to exercise diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of the Municipality’s funds and limit expenditures within the total income realized and collected during the year to avoid incurrence of cash overdraft/deficit; and b) Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to exercise continuing diligence, prudence and proper control and planning that all projects included in the continuing appropriations will not be implemented with no cash available at the end of CY 2019.
observations.managementAction: The Local Finance Committee conducted a meeting and agreed that collections should be monitored. The Local Finance is planning that all projects included in the continuing appropriations with no cash available will not be implemented. Request for SB ratification/approval yet prepared.
observations.reason: The Municipality did not achieved the target collections for the CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to verify and evaluate the authenticity of the above transactions and prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the adjustments for these checks to present an accurate and reliable balance of the Cash in Bank accounts.
observations.managementAction: Prepared Journal Entry Voucher No. 2019-05 dated May 31, 2019 to deduct ₱120,302.76. General Journal dated February 2018 deducted ₱174,437.48.
observations.reason: ₱120,302.76 still formed part of stale checks as of December 31, 2019. Reiterated on Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a) Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers with their corresponding amount due and collectible for the CY 2019 and years thereafter; b) Municipal Accountant to record collection of receivables based on gross amounts of taxes collected and set-up receivables based on the total collectibles for the year as provided by the Municipal Treasurer; c) Municipal Accountant to maintain individual subsidiary ledger card of taxpayers to ensure accuracy and reliability in the recognition of the RPT receivables; and d) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the list of delinquent real property tax as of year-end against the recorded RPT and SET Receivables to present an accurate and reliable receivable accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer provided the certified list of taxpayers at the beginning of the year. The Municipal Treasurer stated that notable amounts constitute prior years’ receivables already forwarded to Provincial Government for action. Office of the Municipal Treasurer adheres to reconcile the list of delinquent real property tax as of yearend against the recorded RPT and SET Receivables. Municipal Accountant has Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) for reconciliation with Accounting Office.
observations.reason: Notable amounts constituted prior years’ receivables including amounts under court litigation already forwarded to Provincial Government. Unreconciled amounts remained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a) effect the necessary adjustments in the municipality’s financial statements in order to have a reconciled balance of reciprocal accounts; b) determine the nature of the amount presented in Other Receivables for receivables from SEF/GF amounting to ₱49,348.60; and c) closely supervise personnel in charge for the proper keeping and classification of accounts for the proper classification of other funds.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entry JEV No. 2019-12 dated December 31, 2019 recognized PPEs purchased from Trust Fund on CY 2017 to 2019. Adjusting entry JEV No. 2019-04-005 dated April 25, 2019 recognized Due from Other Funds. Adjusting entry JEV No. 2019-04-006 dated April 25, 2019 corrected classification of accounts.
observations.reason: There were still reciprocal accounts that were not made in the General Fund; some entries corrected but remaining unreconciled amounts due to records and classification issues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a) recognize the yearly amortization under current portion of Loans Payable-Domestic and properly disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the nature of this account; and b) effect the necessary adjustment for the understatement in recording loan to present a reliable financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Yearly amortization of ₱18,510,618.72 under current portion of Loans Payable was recognized. Corrected the understatement in recording of loan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to monitor the expenses and strictly observe the provisions of law and rules and regulations in the use of public funds particularly Section 325(h), Article I, Chapter III of Republic Act No. 7160. We also required the submission of justification/explanation for the difference noted in the number of rooms as well as the submission of the attendance sheet for the 230 participants each day to validate reliability and correctness of the cost of meals paid by the municipality. Refrain from disbursing municipal fund which is not in accordance with Section 335 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The municipality recognized the issue and monitored expenses. No justification/explanation provided by concerned municipal officials.
observations.reason: No justification/explanation was submitted by the concerned municipal officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) Municipal Mayor to propose policy/guideline in the billing and collection of electric consumption of various stallholders and national government agencies occupying the stalls in the market for separate billing; b) Market Administrator to make representation with BATELEC I for separate billing of stallholders’ electric consumption as they have their own sub meters so payments can be made directly to the latter, if feasible; c) Market Administrator to maintain a subsidiary ledger card for all stallholders to effectively monitor the electric consumption, payments and other fees required from them; and d) Accounting Office to require from the Market Administrator the monthly list of stallholders with corresponding amount of electric consumption and the amount paid so that the former can separately record the receivables pertaining to the stallholders.
observations.managementAction: No policy/guideline was made for the billing and collection of stallholders at the Balayan Mall Type Market. Former Market Administrator made representation with BATELEC for separate billing of stallholders. Market Administrator maintained subsidiary ledger card for each stallholder. Accounting Office to require monthly list from Market management; monthly list will be provided starting CY 2020.
observations.reason: BATELEC did not agree with the proposed policy. Limited resources and difficulty in perfecting separate billing; implementation ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a) Municipal Mayor, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer submit justification/explanation why payments were made without an approved appropriation; b) Municipal Accountant to see to it that all disbursements were covered with appropriation; and c) Market Administrator to strictly observe pertinent provisions of the Municipality’s Revenue Code and Sangguniang Bayan Resolution on market fees and charges required from market stallholders.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant see to it that all disbursements were covered with appropriations. Market Administrator ensured market stallholders observe revenue code and SB resolution.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the erroneous entry of the Small Town Lottery (STL) share to Other Income-Share from PCSO instead of Trust Liability-Due to LGUs in the Trust Fund to present the correct year-end balance of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: The PCSO shares were covered with Memorandum of Agreement which was submitted to the audit team.
observations.reason: No reason provided by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer and the concerned departments to strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 48 of GAA in order to avoid the no recurrence of similar lapses and/or violations in the future. Also, instruct the personnel assigned in the processing of loan applications of employees to make thorough review and computation, particularly its compliance on threshold requirement on net take home pay amount. The Municipality must not accommodate deductions of loan through payroll if such would violate the aforesaid provision of the law.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Human Resource Management Officer issued Memorandum No. 2019-05-047 stating that starting June 1, 2019 the ₱5,000.00 net take home policy will be strictly observed. Some employees had applied loans before implementation.
observations.reason: There were employees who issued loans prior to implementation; confirmation of compliance being enforced starting June 1, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned officials to strictly observe the provisions of Section 321 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Article 417 of the Act Implementing Rules and Regulations and promote the Code objectives of discouraging frequent changes in the preparation of the annual budget or any practice that undermines the integration of planning and budgeting at the local level.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Management commented that it is difficult to make a perfect budget where no realignment was done since the municipality has limited funds to finance various programs.
observations.reason: Limited resources of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Technical Working Group (TWG) to scrutinize the contractor’s manpower on all the projects to be awarded and not on an individual project only in cases where the latter is the lowest bidder in several projects which have the same or overlapping implementation period; b) Municipal Engineer to strictly and closely monitor all projects closely implemented by the contractors and prepare an individual status report per project to be submitted to the Honorable Mayor for monitoring purposes and in order to check, verify and evaluate the quality of work and efficiency of the said contractors; c) Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to provide justification on delayed completion and submit duly approved Suspension Order; and d) Municipal Accountant to impose liquidated damages on the claim of contractors when necessary.
observations.managementAction: A letter dated June 26, 2019 explained that for projects that were awarded according to the provisions of RA 9184, augmentation is subject to project supervision. The Municipal Engineer is monitoring all projects implemented by the municipality. Suspension Orders were issued where applicable. Liquidated damages were imposed where applicable.
observations.reason: Some projects were supervised and monitored; suspension orders and liquidated damages were applied where necessary; some delays due to capacity and supervision issues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to submit the Procurement Monitoring Report to the GPPB as required in Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Procurement Monitoring submitted the Procurement Monitoring Report to GPPB in printed and electronic format within the prescribed period in accordance with the provision of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer, the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and other concerned officials to submit at the beginning of the year the lists of all ongoing government projects and those to be implemented during the year, in compliance with the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator submitted the list of ongoing government projects and those to be implemented at the beginning of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: a) MDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMFIP and Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in the prescribed format and submit on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through LDRRMC and Local Development Council; b) Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF-MOOE and QRF for CY 2019 amounting to ₱8,156,416.23 in the Trust Fund books; c) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to effect the necessary journal entries in the accounting books and Registry of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligations for the reversion of LDRRMF amounting to ₱15,138,092.73 lodged in the trust fund books which remained unutilized for more than five years; d) Direct the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to charge only eligible items against the fund and ensure that charges to Quick Response Fund are supported with Sangguniang Bayan Resolution declaring state of calamity; and e) MDRRMO to submit the Sangguniang Bayan Resolution declaring state of calamity for Bagyong Tisoy to the audit team for verification.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer commented that they will adhere to observe the necessary journal entries in the RAAO for the ensuing years pertaining to the reversion of LDRRM Fund for more than five years. Previous LDRRM Fund reverted to trust fund were utilized according to the pertinent provisions of RA 10121. They will ensure charges only on eligible items against LDRRM Fund and that charges to QRF for the ensuing years are supported with SB Resolution declaring state of calamity. According to report of previous MDRRMO, purchase of welfare goods amounting to ₱82,947.50 charged to Mitigation Fund was for stockpile/reserve for disaster preparedness.
observations.reason: Some submissions were made upon request; reversion and transfer actions to be undertaken and journal entries to be effected; some unutilized balances remain requiring reversion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person and Municipal Budget Officer to prepare GAD budget in accordance with the JMC and ensure implementation. Also require the GAD Focal Person to submit to the Audit Team copy of the approved GPB to facilitate review and evaluation of the plan and reported accomplishments.
observations.managementAction: The Local Finance Committee agreed to reasonably project income collectible and to impose more effort on tax collection. GAD Focal Person to strive for full implementation of GAD-related PPAs by maximizing utilization of the amount set aside. No copy of approved GPB submitted.
observations.reason: Limited funding; only 56.25% of the budget utilized during the year; approved GPB not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipality to exert more effort to strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9003 to ensure the protection of public health and the environment and continuous operation of closed/open dump site be discontinued and proper closure undertaken. Prepare and approve the Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan and strictly implement the closure of open dump site.
observations.managementAction: Controlled dumpsite was completely closed during CY 2019. Follow up made by MENRO last September 2019. Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan was still not yet approved by National Solid Waste Management Board despite follow-up.
observations.reason: Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan was still not yet approved by National Solid Waste Management Board despite follow-up; closure of dumpsite achieved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer effect the cancellation of the stale checks and the Municipal Accountant make proper adjustments in the municipality’s books of accounts to fairly present its financial statements as of December 31, 2017.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer coordinated with the Municipal Accountant for the adjustment of stale checks to Accounts Payable. The former also added that the payee was notified if ever the check was stale already and required the submission of the original check for replacement. However, there are times that the checks were already in the possession of the payee and these were treated as outstanding checks.
observations.reason: Some checks were already cancelled and issued to the payees but were not yet negotiated with the bank.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant expedite the submission of the request for write-off of these dormant accounts to the Commission on Audit supported with Sangguniang Bayan Resolution requesting the write-off and certification as to extent of management validation on these accounts, aging schedule and available records pertaining to these dormant accounts, if any.
observations.managementAction: No available records to support the request for write-off. The Municipal Accountant was advised to prepare required documentation.
observations.reason: No records available to support write-off; pending management validation and Sangguniang Bayan resolution.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with the Municipal General Services Officer to determine the details of
the beginning balances of the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) from their
oldest available records for proper accountability and adjustment of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services and
Accounting Offices to make periodic reconciliation of the PPE accounts to present
a more reliable financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
recognize in the books the unrecorded assets arising from Trust Fund for the
current and prior periods.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal General Services
Officer to have an updated list of unserviceable items for disposal and prepare all
the necessary documents/reports to act on the properties to be disposed within the
reasonable time to prevent further deterioration of the PPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Local Finance Committee to adopt strategy in order to increase
revenue/collections and monitor properly the availability of cash every
month to ensure that all income due the Municipality be collected in full
and assure continuous flow of funds; and
b. Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to
exercise diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of the
Municipality’s funds and limit expenditures within the total income realized
and collected during the year to avoid incurrence of cash overdraft/deficit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant verify and evaluate the nature and
authenticity of the above transactions and prepare the necessary Journal
Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the adjustments for these checks to present an
accurate and reliable balance of the Cash in Bank accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant the certified
list of taxpayers with their corresponding amount due and collectible for the CY
2019 and years thereafter;
b) Municipal Accountant to record collection of receivables based on
gross amounts of taxes collected and set-up receivables based on the total
collectibles for the year as provided by the Municipal Treasurer; and
c) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the list of
delinquent real property tax as of year-end against the recorded RPT and SET
Receivables to present an accurate and reliable receivable accounts in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
 Effect the necessary adjustments in the municipality’s financial
statements in order to have a reconciled balance of reciprocal
accounts;
 Determine the nature of the amount presented in Other Receivables for
the receivables from SEF/GF amounting to P49,348.60; and
 Closely supervise personnel in charge in keeping the accounts for
General Fund, Special Education /fund and Trust Fund to ensure the
correctness and accuracy in the recording of transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to:
 Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer regarding the payment made by the
Steel Corporation of the Philippines to effect the necessary adjustments and to
fully disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the actual status of said payment;
 Reconcile their records with the Provincial Accountant and settle discrepancies
between the balances of reciprocal accounts so that the Municipality will be able to
present the correct balances of inter-agency receivables and payables in the
financial statements;
 Furnish the Provincial Accountant with the copies of the JEVs showing the amount
due to LGU as the basis for recording the collections made by the Municipality in the
books of the Province and if adjustment/correction was made in the province
regarding the collections made by the Municipality;
 analyze fully the nature of each transaction and reclassify to appropriate accounts so
as not to mislead the users of the financial statements; and
 ascertain the nature of the recorded amount of P368,824.56 and if found that it is not
valid, a request from the Commission on Audit be secured for the de-recognition
and/or adjustment of the dormant account balances in the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
 Recognize the yearly amortization of P18,510,618.72 under current liability
for account Loans Payable-Domestic and properly disclose in the Notes to
Financial Statements the nature of this account; and
 Effect the necessary adjustment for the understatement in recording loan to
present a reliable financial statements.

We again recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to monitor the expenses and strictly observe provisions of law and
rules and regulations in the use of public funds particularly Section 325(h), Article
I, Chapter III of Republic Act No. 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991.
We also required the submission of justification/explanation for the difference
noted in the number of rooms as well as the submission of the attendance sheet
for the 230 participants each day to validate reliability and correctness of the cost
of meals paid by the municipality. Refrain from disbursing municipal fund which is
not in accordance with Section 335 of RA 7160.

We recommended that the:
 Municipal Mayor to propose policy/guideline in the billing and collection of
electric consumption of various stallholders as well as the national
government agencies occupying the stalls in the market for approval by the
Sangguniang Bayan through resolution/ordinance;
 Market Administrator to make representation with BATELEC I for the
separate billing of stallholders’ electric consumption as they have their
own sub meters so payments can be made directly to the latter, if feasible;
 Market Administrator to maintain a subsidiary ledger card for all
stallholders to effectively monitor the electric consumption, payments and
other fees required from them. The income to be recognized should be the
additional mark up on the number of kilowatt consumed; and
 Accounting Office to require from the Market Administrator the monthly list
of stallholders with corresponding amount of electric consumption and the
amount paid so that the former can separately record the receivables
pertaining to the stallholders.

We recommended that the:
 Municipal Mayor, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit
justification/explanation why payments were made without an approved
appropriation;
 Municipal Accountant to see to it that all disbursements were covered with
appropriation; and
 Market Administrator to strictly observe pertinent provisions of the
Municipality’s Revenue Code and SB Resolution regarding the fees and
charges required from market stallholders.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Journal Entry
Voucher (JEV) to reclassify the erroneous entry of the Small Town Lottery (STL)
share to Other Income-Share from PCSO instead of Trust Liability-Due to LGUs
in the Trust Fund to present the correct year-end balance of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Human
Resource Management Officer and the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere
with the provisions of Section 48 of 2018 GAA in order to avoid the recurrence of
same lapses and/or violations in similar forthcoming transactions, particularly
that the GAA for the ensuing year also contained similar provision for the
retention of a higher minimum net take home pay. Further, instruct the personnel
assigned in the processing of loan applications of employees to make thorough
review and computation, particularly its compliance on threshold requirement on
net take home pay amount. The Municipality must not accommodate deductions
of loan through payroll if such would violate the aforesaid provision of the law.

We again recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Honorable Members of
the Sangguniang Bayan strictly observe the provisions of Section 321 of Republic
Act No. 7160 and Article 417 of the Act Implementing Rules and Regulations and
promote the Code objectives of discouraging frequent changes in the preparation
of the annual budget or any practice that undermines the integration of planning
and budgeting at the local level.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a) Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group (TWG) to
scrutinize the contractor’s manpower on all projects to be awarded and not on
an individual project only in cases where the latter is the lowest bidder in
several projects which have the same or overlapping implementation period.
This is in order to require contractor to augment its manpower to be utilized in
the project awarded to it or to disqualify it from award of several contracts
beyond its capacity;
b) Municipal Engineer to strictly and closely monitor all projects simultaneously
implemented by the contractors and prepare an individual status report per
project to be submitted to the Honorable Mayor for monitoring purposes and in
order to check, verify and evaluate the quality of work and efficiency of the
said contractors; and
c) Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to
provide justification on the delayed completion of the above-mentioned
projects and submit duly approved Suspension Order; and
d) Municipal Accountant to impose liquidated damages on the claim of
contractors of the above-mentioned projects when necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) to submit the approved Procurement Monitoring Report to the
GPPB in printed and electronic format within the prescribed period in accordance
with the provision of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer, the
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and other concerned officials to
submit at the beginning of the year the lists of all ongoing government
projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented during
the year, in compliance with the above cited regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to prepare the Local Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) and
Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF as required in the
aforementioned regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer to judiciously plan on how to maximize the
utilization of the LDRRM Funds and to accelerate their implementation in order
to achieve the goal/target of the Municipality regarding disaster relief, recovery
and risk reduction and management.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
revert the LDRRMF unutilized balances for CYs 2012 and 2013 to the unappropriated
surplus of the General Fund to be made available for other social services after
subsequent enactment by the Local Council.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
transfer the unexpended balance of LDRRMF for MOOE only and the unexpended
balance for Capital Outlay be retained in the General Fund as continuing
appropriation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
see to it that all disbursement of Quick Response Fund be supported with
Sangguniang Resolution declaring the state of calamity.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose in the Notes to the
Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended balance of
LDRRMF in order to inform the users of relevant information with the prescribed
format.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
 GAD Focal Person and concerned officials and employees involved in the
preparation of budget to see to it that the funding requirement for GAD is
fully complied with;
 GAD Focal Person to strive for the full implementation of GAD-related
PPAs by maximizing the utilization of the amount set aside for said
purpose so that the desired outcomes are attained and benefits are
enjoyed to the fullest by the intended beneficiaries; and
 GAD Focal Person to submit to the audit team a copy of the approved GPB
to facilitate review of the same.

We again recommended that the Municipal Government of Balayan exert more
effort to strictly comply with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003 to ensure
the protection of public health and the environment.

1. The Inventory Team conduct physical inventory-taking of all the LGU’s property, plant and equipment and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). Submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) for CY-2018 to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year.  
2. The Municipal Accountant reconcile with the submitted RPCI of the GSO. Also, apply consistently the perpetual inventory accounting method on all supplies and materials procurement of the municipality to fairly present the inventory and expense accounts in the financial statements.  
3. Immediately comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the local roads asset management system provided in COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction:
Submitted the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than March 15,
2019.
The Accounting Office furnished the GSO/ GSO Ledger Cards and Property
Cards, respectively, to facilitate the reconciliation of both records.
However, there were still PPE accounts that were not reconciled.
observations.reason: Prior year’s balance had no detail as to the description, date of acquisition and whereabouts of property.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality observe strictly the auditing rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. Disbursing Officers shall be designated and their fidelity be insured in the fidelity fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality made it a point that excess in management. The Disbursing Officers handling all advances made by the Office of the Mayor and Ms. Norilyn Miranda were designated as Special Disbursing Officer- Disbursing Officer and they were bonded.

observations.recommendation:
1. The Municipal GSO Conduct inventory of the physical supplies and prepare the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI). Submit copy of RPCI to the Municipal Accountant and to the Audit Team within the prescribed period and reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records and establish the causes of discrepancies, if any.
2. The Municipal Accountant reconcile with the submitted RPCI of the GSO with the records of accounting. Also, apply consistently the perpetual inventory accounting method on all supplies and materials procurement of the municipality to fairly present the inventory and expense accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI). The Accounting Office furnished the GSO with the records of accounting. However, no reconciliation was made.
observations.reason: No RPCI was prepared by the GSO. Non conduct of physical count of inventories.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review and revise Note 27 of the Notes to Financial Statements of the Municipality of Balayan for CY 2017 and effect necessary corrections to show the correct amounts in each elements of Note 27 or Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/(Deficit) CY 2017.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the revised and final copies of the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor convene the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) quarterly and to prepare the annual Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) for CY 2017 and submit to the Commission; and that the Municipal Accountant transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of Quick Response Fund and Local Disaster Risk Management (LDRRMF) – Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses for CY 2017 totaling ₱5,856,139.72 to the Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund books and maintain subsidiary ledgers for transfers of agency’s unutilized LDRRMF to the special trust fund by year of transfer.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO has ensured compliance with the audit recommendation. The Local Disaster Risk Management Officer and the other concerned local government officials have been advised to observe strictly the provisions of RA No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the operation and accounting of LDRRM Fund.
observations.reason: Being the designated Municipal Administrator as an additional assignment, he was not able to fully observe the provisions of said regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipality carefully consider the noted plans that the municipality included in the Annual Budget serve as a planned resource guide in the allocation and implementation of PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality made it a point that excess in the PS Limitation will be avoided. The Municipality shall operate within the budget constraint and ensure that the local funds are allocated adequately for development projects and for the delivery of local basic services.
observations.reason: No comment offered by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to monitor the expenses and strictly observe provisions of law and rules and regulations in the use of public funds particularly Section 325(h), Article I, Chapter III of Republic Act No. 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Administrator reasoned out that the amount spent was actually for the promotion of Tourism Industry of the LGU where the culture and tradition in various programs and activities were presented. On the other hand, he also added that the Grand Surgical Mission for CY 2018 is a five year program/activity of which initial implementation was made this year. As such, there were lapses in the documentation and how the activity implemented. Nevertheless, the municipal officials concerned are willing to abide with the rules and regulations regarding this matter.
observations.reason: The municipality increased its Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses to P2,875,300.00 for CY 2018; lapses in documentation and implementation remain.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality carefully consider the noted transactions with Lucky Charm’s Trading. We likewise recommended that sanctions be imposed on individuals committing violations in the performance of the functions of the Bids and Awards Committee. Also, please be reminded that all violations committed against the provisions of RA 9184 are subject of audit suspension/disallowance. Ensure that the procedures of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 are being followed.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2018, purchases were made from Lucky Charm’s Trading without strict observance of the provisions of RA 9184. Lucky Charm’s updated with BIR on their line of business.
observations.reason: Not all suppliers within the locality were registered with PhilGEPS; limited competition; hesitant to join the bidding; Lucky Charm’s updated BIR but procurement procedures lacked full compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality refrain from the practice of reimbursement and consider the Memorandum of Agreement entered into by the LGU with Balayan Shell Gasoline Station. Ensure that the procedures prescribed therein are being followed.
observations.managementAction: There was no justification submitted to the audit team. The municipality still uses alternative method of procurement for some transactions. No additional action provided.
observations.reason: Late preparation of the financial statements. Unavailability of local suppliers to avoid reimbursement; some practices continued.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board enforce the strict compliance of the provisions of Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017 of the Department of Education, Department of Budget and Management and Department of Interior and Local Government to ensure that Special Education Fund (SEF) will be utilized for the intended purpose or according to the identified priority areas listed in the AIP; submit the Annual AIP prior to approval of the annual budget.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2018, the local school board had their AIP; however there were times that priority projects cropped up and to be attended immediately. For the coming school year, a comprehensive AIP will be submitted prior to approval of their budget.
observations.reason: Limited fund for implementation; priorities cropped up requiring adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Honorable Members of the Sangguniang Bayan strictly observe the provisions of Section 321 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Article 417 of the Act Implementing Rules and Regulations and promote the Code objectives of discouraging frequent changes in the preparation of the annual budget or any practice that undermines the integration of planning and budgeting at the local level.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer commented that it was difficult to make a perfect budget where no realignment/augmentation will be made since the municipality has limited fund to finance various programs/projects/activities and other expenditures. She also added that these realignments/augmentation were supported with approved SB Resolutions.
observations.reason: Management view: difficult to avoid realignments due to limited funds; supported by SB Resolutions. Audit view: realignments should be minimized with proper planning.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality carefully consider the noted plans that the municipality included in the Annual Budget serve as a planned resource guide in the allocation and implementation of PPAs. Ensure proper planning and that approved plans are operationalized.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality noted that approved local development plans and programs are the basis but implementation depends on availability of funds. The PNB loan amount approved was ₱250 Million but only ₱16,518,320.00 was released and used for purchase of lot.
observations.reason: Limited releases of approved loan; only initial release made; thus implementation constrained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the full and optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund which is primarily intended to the projects that will contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes. Ensure itemized projects, avoid charging ordinary repairs to the fund contrary to Joint Circular No. 2017-1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator commented that projects were identified but there were realignments. Some projects were implemented in the 1st quarter of CY 2019.
observations.reason: There were identified projects but realignments and delays; some projects implemented in 1st quarter CY2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor immediately implement the programs and projects under the Assistance to Disadvantaged Municipalities (ADM) Program for the benefit of the constituents and ensure proper use thereof in accordance with the guidelines under the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-3.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality indicated that projects were planned and some were implemented; compliance with DILG-DBM JMC was being observed.
observations.reason: There were delays in utilization and implementation; some projects later implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality not only reorganized the Balayan Local Council for the Protection of Children (BLCPC) but also activate, strengthen, and sustain the organized BLCPC. Allocate the mandatory one percent of internal revenue allotment and prioritize the Council’s activities including preparation of Annual Work and Financial Plan, Local Development Plan for Children, Local Investment Plan and other required documents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality organized and conducted training and orientation of local council and prepared the required plans. The LGU made attribution and implemented activities for LCPC.
observations.reason: Limited fund for LCPC activities; some actions taken but full functionality not yet sustained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality exert more effort to strictly comply with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003 and pursue necessary actions to finalize and submit the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan and close/convert open dumps to proper facilities.
observations.managementAction: The designated MENRO commented that his Office has no enough manpower to update the ten-year plan. He also mentioned that the municipality is planning to contract the hauling services which will cost million in a matter of 35 days.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel and resources; plan not yet updated or approved by the Commission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU prepare, review, approve and allocate the mandatory five (5) percent of the total annual budget to support GAD activities; submit required documents to COA.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO) commented that she will seek the assistance of various Department Heads in order to comply with the audit team’s recommendation.
observations.reason: No prior full compliance; allocation increased but still below 5%; submission of GPB to COA delayed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the municipality’s year-end financial statements and supporting schedules as required.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted several financial statements; however, some barangays did not submit their financial statements within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Late or non-submission by 48 barangays; incomplete barangay-level compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor include among its top priorities the construction of a building for the safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of documents generated by the various departments/offices and ensure consideration on the preservation period of ten (10) years as mandated by law.
observations.managementAction: A ground breaking ceremony for the construction of government center for the Municipality of Balayan has been done. The Municipality purchased a lot and conducted ground breaking.
observations.reason: Work plan not yet fully implemented at the time; construction initiated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant accurately present reconciliation of the SCBAA versus the Statement of Financial Performance and ensure the comparability and consistency between budget and actual figures.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting and Budget Office provided reconciliation statements and some adjustments were made; however, full reconciliation of all items still pending.
observations.reason: Coordination issues between Accounting and Budget Office; late adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor cause the production of all the lacking documents to facilitate the issuance of Tax Declaration and Certificate of Title/Transfer Certificate of Title in the name of the Municipality and to strictly comply with the requirements on the procurement of land as mandated in Section 13 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and pertinent provision of PD No. 1529.
observations.managementAction: The Deed of Absolute was annotated. The Municipality coordinated with DENR. Some documents were produced however the full titling process delayed. Acquisition used loan proceeds; required DENR documents not yet all obtained.
observations.reason: Delay in processing and obtaining DENR/other required certificates; some documents produced but titling not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Honorable Members of the Sanggunian include among its top priorities the strict observance of Joint Circular No. 2015-01 issued by the Commission on Audit and Department of Budget and Management and Department of the Interior and Local Government and other related issuances. Failure or refusal to do so shall constitute ground for administrative disciplinary action as well as penal sanctions under existing laws.
observations.managementAction: No comment/justification submitted. Municipality acknowledged recommendation and committed to comply.
observations.reason: Lack of complete supporting documents and justifications; some expenses remain subject to review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide and maintain a complete set of subsidiary ledgers for special accounts and prepare and submit financial statements as required in Section 313 of RA 7160 and Sections 107 and 110 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared and maintained subsidiary ledgers for several special accounts; financial statements were prepared but not all subsidiary ledgers were submitted to the audit team.
observations.reason: Late preparation and submission of some subsidiary ledgers and financial statements to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant expedite the submission of the request for write-off of these dormant accounts to the Commission on Audit supported with Sangguniang Bayan Resolution requesting the same and certification as to extent of management validation on these accounts, aging schedule and available records pertaining to these dormant accounts, if any.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the full and optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund which is primarily intended to fund projects that shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes. We further recommended the strict observance of Article 454(d) of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 7160 in order to identify clearly the specific programs, projects and activities prioritized each budget year.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented
observations.reason: The 20 percent development fund in CY 2017 remained with unutilized balance equivalent to 41% of the statutory appropriation for CY 2017 and lump sum amounts were observed in the Annual Investment Program also in the same year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enforce full implementation of the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003 otherwise known as the “Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000” in order to protect the environment and the health of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office (MENRO) mentioned that the 10-year plan is for updating and the open dump site is set for safe closure this 2018. He added that they have already received assistance from the Environmental Management Bureau for its closure.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor to cause the production of all the lacking documentations and facilitate the immediate issuance of Tax Declaration and Certificate of Title/Transfer Certificate of Title in the name of the Municipality. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor remind the Municipal Accountant to refrain from paying government transaction without complete documentation and to strictly comply with the requirements on the procurement of land as mandated in Section 13 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and pertinent provision of Presidential Decree No. 1529.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and concerned officials have not yet completed the required ownership documentations. They promised to comply in CY 2018.
observations.reason: The required ownership documentations were not yet completed or were not yet transferred and remained untitled.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the material discrepancy of the PPE accounts and continuous
failure of the LGU to correct such deficiencies, we recommended that the LGU
consider the following:
1. The Inventory Team conduct complete inventory-taking of all the LGU’s
properties and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant
and Equipment to be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than
January 31 of each year; and
2. The Municipal Accountant and the GSO/Property Officer maintain complete
and updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, to facilitate
the reconciliation of both records.
3. Immediately comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines provided in
the Commission on Audit Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 to
fairly present the LGU’s financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer effect the cancellation of the
stale checks and the Municipal Accountant make proper adjustments in the
municipality’s books of accounts to fairly present its financial statements as of
December 31, 2017.

We recommended that the Municipality observe strictly the auditing rules
and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
Likewise, Disbursing Officers shall be designated and shall their fidelity be
insured in the fidelity fund.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require
the following:
1. The Municipal GSO Conduct the physical count of inventories every
semester and prepare the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI).
Submit copy of RPCI to the Municipal Accountant and to the Audit Team
within the prescribed period and reconcile the results of the count with the
accounting records and establish the causes of discrepancies, if any.
2. The Municipal Accountant reconcile with the submitted RPCI of the GSO
with the records of accounting. Also, apply consistently the perpetual
48
inventory accounting method on all supplies and materials procurement of
the municipality to fairly present the inventory and expense accounts in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to review and revise Note 27 of the Notes to Financial Statements of the
financial statements of the Municipality of Balayan for CY 2017 and effect
necessary corrections to show the correct amounts in each elements of Note 27 or
Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/(Deficit) CY
2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the LDRRM Officer
and other concerned officials to observe strictly the provisions of Republic Act No.
10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor convene the Local Disaster
Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) quarterly and to prepare the
annual Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Investment Plan
(LDRRMFIP).

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of Quick Response
Fund and Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) –
Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses for CY 2017 totaling P5,856,139.72
and submit to the Commission within the prescribed period the Report on Sources
and Utilization of LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipality observe strictly the provisions of
Section 325(a) of Republic Act No. 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991.
The Municipality shall operate within the budgetary constraint of the PS limitation
and ensure that the local funds are allocated adequately for development projects
and for the delivery of local basic services. We further recommend that the LGU
refrain from the commission of similar violation which would be subject of audit
disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to monitor the expenses and strictly observe provisions of law and rules
and regulations in the use of public funds particularly Section 325(h), Article I,
Chapter III of Republic Act No. 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) to explain the various transactions made with the
Lucky Charm’s Trading without the strict observance of the provisions of RA 9184.

We likewise recommended that sanctions be imposed on individuals seriously
committing violations in the performance of the functions of the Bids and Awards
Committee. Also, please be reminded that all violations committed against the
provisions of RA 9184 are subject of audit
suspension/disallowance. Basically, the LGU procurement process shall
promote competitiveness, transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency, and
economy.

We recommended the Municipality submit justification on the noted
inconsistencies with the transaction.

We also recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee strictly observe
the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in using the alternative method of
procurement. Ensure that the procedures prescribed therein are being followed.

We also recommended that the Municipal Vice Mayor refrain from the
practice of reimbursement and consider the Memorandum of Agreement entered
into by the LGU with Balayan Shell Gasoline Station.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Local School Board
enforce the strict compliance of the provisions of Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017
of the Department of Education, Department of Budget and Management and
Department of the Interior and Local Government to ensure that Special Education
Fund (SEF) will be utilized for the intended purpose or according to the identified
priority areas listed in the Annual Implementation Plan (AIP) culled from the
DepEd-approved School Improvement Plan (SIP). Successful planning and
implementation are factors essential to effective governance which results in the
effective delivery of basic education services.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Honorable Members of
the Sangguniang Bayan strictly observe the provisions of Section 321 of Republic
Act No. 7160 and Article 417 of the Act Implementing Rules and Regulations and
promote the Code objectives of discouraging frequent changes in the preparation
of the annual budget or any practice that undermines the integration of planning
and budgeting at the local level.

We recommended that the Municipality carefully consider the noted
discrepancies between the planned resource allocation and the resulting obligation
of these resources. As previously mentioned, local budgets shall operationalize
approved local development plans, in order to push the planned programs and
projects for the year and to provide the necessary services to the public as a whole.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the full and optimal
utilization of the 20% Development Fund which is primarily intended to fund
projects that shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic
development and environmental management outcomes. Any delay in the
implementation of development projects means late delivery of basic social and
economic services to the municipality’s constituents and to slow down community
development or realization of development objectives.

We further recommended the strict observance of Article 454(d) of the
Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 7160 that disbursements from special
accounts shall proceed from itemized appropriation which shall be for specific
development projects/activities embodied in the local development plan and/or
public investment program formulated and prioritized by the Local Development
Council and approved by the Sanggunian concerned.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning
and Development Coordinator to immediately and fully implement the programs and
projects under the Assistance to Disadvantaged Municipalities (ADM) for the
benefit of the constituents and ensure proper use thereof in accordance with the
guidelines under the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-3.

We recommended that the Municipality not only reorganized the Balayan
Local Council for the Protection of Children (BLCPC) but also activate, strengthen,
and sustain the already organized BLCPC. Allocate the mandatory budget of one
(1) percent of internal revenue allotment and prioritize in the Council’s activities the
preparation of Annual Work and Financial Plan, Local Development Plan for
Children, Local Investment Plan for Children, Local Code for Children, Local State
of Children’s Report, Children’s Profile and the establishment, maintenance and
updating of data base on children for the strengthening and proper implementation
of the programs of the council.

We recommended that the Municipal Government of Balayan exert more
effort to strictly comply with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003. The
Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office (MENRO) should ensure
that the public has high level of awareness on R.A 9003 thru distribution of
flyers/leaflets, conducting campaigns and seminars in every barangays which in
effect shall educate the public in every way possible regarding the importance of
solid waste management, the benefits of complying with the said law, the
detrimental effect of garbage to one’s health, the advantages and disadvantages of
having and not having a Material Recovery Facility (MRF) and legal implications of
non-compliance with the Act.

We recommended that the Municipality implement strictly provisions of
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 and 2013-01 of Philippine Commission on
Women, Department of the Interior and Local Government, Department of Budget
and Management and National Economic and Development Authority to plan,
budget and prepare accomplishment report as prescribed; submit these documents
to Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) for GAD PPAs alignment
and to DILG Provincial Office for its review and endorsement and allocate the
mandatory at least five (5) percent of the total budget appropriations to support
GAD PPAs.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant fully comply with the
aforementioned law, rules and regulations of government accounting and auditing
on the submission of the year-end financial statements particularly of the 48
barangays of the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the honorable members of
the Sanggunian include among its top priorities the construction of a building for
the safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of documents
generated by the various departments/offices of the Municipality, particularly those
for submission to the audit team, with consideration on the preservation period of
ten (10) years as mandated by law.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant present accurately the
reconciliation of the budget actual amounts with the statement of financial
performance pursuant to Sections 15 and 21 of Philippine Public Sector Accounting
Standards I – Presentation of Financial Statements.

In view of the material discrepancy of the PPE accounts and continuous failure of the LGU to correct such deficiencies, we recommended that the LGU consider the following:
1. The Inventory Team conduct complete inventory-taking of all the LGU’s properties and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year; and
2. The Municipal Accountant and the GSO/Property Officer maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, to facilitate the reconciliation of both records.
3. Immediately comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines provided in the Commission on Audit Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 to fairly present the LGU’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
