The Municipal Accountant commented that the PPE worth ₱32,926,284.25 without depreciation are public infrastructures recently booked up in the accounts of the municipality while the noted discrepancy with the physical count was pertaining to property, plant and equipment under Special Education Fund (SEF) which were not physically counted as of yearend.
On June 20, 2018, the OIC-GSO submitted a Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment- SEF to reconcile the variance noted by the team. As to the maintenance of PPE Ledger Cards by the Accounting Office and Property Cards by GSO, they cited that the Accounting Office maintains subsidiary ledger of each account. The Municipal Accountant added that they will start reconciling the balance of PPE per item.
observations.reason: The Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment as of December 31, 2017 showed unreconciled balance of ₱101,030,217.25.

observations.recommendation:

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue the demand letter to the concerned officials and employees to immediately settle their unliquidated cash advances and when no liquidation of the previous year’s cash advance is received on or before January 20 of the ensuing year, cause the withholding of the accountable officer’s, official’s or employee’s salary as provided in Section 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant acknowledged the observation and said she will attend to it accordingly.

She added that she will request to the Municipal Accountant to take effect the cancellation of the stale checks and make proper adjustments in the books.
observations.reason: Stale checks under the Special Fund and Education Fund remained not adjusted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to record expenses according to the corresponding accounts prescribed for Local Government Units. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the required supporting documents noted therein and to stop the practice of paying disbursement vouchers with incomplete supporting documents. Strict compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 is highly recommended.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant acknowledged the observation and said she will attend to it accordingly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the required reports and to strictly adhere with the schedules. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the rules on disclosing information in the Notes to Financial Statements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reasoned out that delay in the preparation and submission of barangay financial statements was due to late submission of data and accounts by the barangay official. She also noted that the municipal financial statements were submitted on February 14, 2018.
observations.reason: The Municipality submitted the statements of CY 2016; statements of 48 barangays have not yet been submitted to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality establish guidelines/parameters in identifying the qualified and eligible beneficiaries for the grant of financial assistance in terms of donation by the Municipality to promote uniformity in the amount to be granted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office (MSWDO) and Municipal officials created the guidelines for the donations granted to promote uniformity in the amount granted to individuals.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality implement projects under the Bottom-up-Budgeting (BuB) Program for the development of its constituents immediately and fully. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the disbursement requirements set forth in the DBM-DILG-NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 6 and COA Circular No. 2012-001. Ensure that all claims against government funds be supported with all the required documents. Require submission of the lacking documents and refrain from paying disbursement vouchers with incomplete supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality fully implemented the Bottom-up-Budgeting projects. The Municipal Accountant settled the required supporting documents and ensured payments were supported.
observations.reason: Supporting documents were submitted and claims settled; disbursement vouchers were supported as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require strict compliance of the provisions of Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act of 2010 in the allocation and utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF). We also recommended the submission of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) Sources and Utilization Report to the COA Auditor of the LGU as required in Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

observations.reason: The sources and utilization report has not yet been regularly submitted to the local auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the provisions of Article 210 to 213 and 215 of RA 7160 and Section 3.4 of National Budget Circular No. 563 and that the amount of P69,800.00 be refunded by the concerned employees.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality settled the amount.

Failure or refusal to do so without justifiable cause shall constitute a ground for administrative disciplinary action as well as disallowing the cash advance granted and appropriate penal sanctions under existing laws.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality implemented the required procedures and the disbursement vouchers were supported as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the    efficient and effective utilization of the 20% Development Fund which is    primarily intended to fund projects that shall contribute to the    attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental    management outcomes and strictly observe that disbursements from special    accounts shall proceed from itemized appropriation which shall be for    specific development projects/activities embodied in the local development plan and/or public investment program formulated and prioritized by the Local Development Council and approved by the Sanggunian concerned.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the material discrepancy of the PPE accounts and continuous
failure of the  LGU  to  correct  such  deficiencies,  we  recommended  that
Management consider the following:


 1. The Inventory Team conduct complete inventory-taking of all  the  LGU’s
    properties and prepare  the  Report on the Physical Count of  Property,
    Plant and Equipment to be submitted to the Auditor concerned not  later
    than January 31 of each year; and


 2. The Municipal Accountant and the GSO/Property Officer maintain complete
    and updated PPE Ledger  Cards  and  Property  Cards,  respectively,  to
    facilitate the reconciliation of both records.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  require  all  officers  and
employees of the Municipality to liquidate their cash  advances  within  the
prescribed period.


      We further recommended that  the  Municipal  Accountant  issue  demand
letter to concerned officers  and  employees  to  immediately  settle  their
unliquidated cash advances and when no liquidation of  the  previous  year’s
cash advance is received on or before January 20 of the ensuing year,  cause
the withholding of  the  accountable  officer’s,  official’s  or  employee’s
salary as provided in Section 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated  February
10, 1997.

We recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
Treasurer to effect the cancellation of the stale checks and the  Municipal
Accountant to make  proper  adjustments  in  the  municipality’s  books  of
accounts to fairly present its financial  statements  as  of  December  31,
2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  and  the  Municipal
Treasurer expedite the submission of the  request  for  write-off  of  these
dormant accounts to the  Commission  on  Audit  supported  with  Sangguniang
Bayan Resolution requesting the same  and  certification  as  to  extent  of
management validation  on  these  accounts,  aging  schedule  and  available
records pertaining to these dormant accounts, if any.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the  Municipal
Accountant to  record  expenses  according  to  the  corresponding  accounts
prescribed under the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units.

       Likewise,  we  recommended  that  the  Honorable  Mayor  require  the
Municipal Accountant to immediately  submit  the  required  documents  noted
therein and to stop  the  practice  of  paying  disbursement  vouchers  with
incomplete supporting documents. Strict compliance with Section 4(6)  of  PD
1445 is highly recommended.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct   the   Municipal
Accountant to immediately  submit  the  required  reports  and  to  strictly
adhere with the rules on disclosing information in the  Notes  to  Financial
Statements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.

We advised that the Municipality  establish  guidelines/parameters  in
identifying the qualified  and  eligible  beneficiaries  for  the  grant  of
financial assistance in terms of donation by  the  Municipality  to  promote
uniformity in the amount to be granted.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  ensure  the  efficient  and
effective utilization  of  the  20%  Development  Fund  which  is  primarily
intended to fund  projects  that  shall  contribute  to  the  attainment  of
desirable socio-economic development.

      We  further  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  instill   among
concerned  officials  the  strict  observance  of  Article  454(d)  of   the
Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 7160 in order to  identify  clearly
the specific programs,  projects  and  activities  prioritized  each  budget
year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  enforce  full  implementation
of  the  provisions  of  Republic  Act  No.  9003  otherwise  known  as  the
Philippine Ecological  Solid  Waste  Management  Act  of  2000  in  order  to
protect the environment and the health of the constituents.

We recommended that the Municipality implement immediately  and  fully
programs/projects  under  the  Bottom-up-Budgeting  (BuB)  Program  for  the
development of its constituents.  We also  recommended  that  the  Municipal
Accountant strictly comply with the requirements set forth in the  DBM-DILG-
DSWD-NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 6 and COA Cir. No. 2012-001.  Ensure
that all claims against government funds be supported with all the  required
documents. Require submission of the  lacking  documents  and  refrain  from
paying disbursement vouchers with incomplete supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Assessor to cause the  production  of  all  the
lacking documents to facilitate the immediate issuance  of  Tax  Declaration
and Certificate of Title/Transfer Certificate of Title in the  name  of  the
Municipality.

       Likewise,  we  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  remind   the
Municipal Accountant to refrain from paying  government transaction  without
complete documentation and to strictly comply with the requirements  on  the
procurement of land as mandated in Section 13 of COA Circular  No.  2012-001
and pertinent  provision of Presidential Decree No. 1529.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require strict  compliance  of
the provisions of Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management  Act  of
2010 in the allocation and utilization of the Local Disaster Risk  Reduction
and Management Fund (LDRRMF ).


      We also recommended to submit the Local Disaster  Risk  Reduction  and
Management Fund (LDRRMF ) Sources and Utilization Report the COA Auditor  of
the LGU as required in Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to strictly observe provisions of Article 210 to 213 and  215  of
RA 7160 and Section 3.4 of National Budget Circular No. 563 April 22,  2016.
 Contributions and elective officials expenses related to leagues which  are
not included in the preceding  articles  are  not  government  expenses  and
should not  be  charged  against  the  funds  of  the  municipality.      In
addition, the previously mentioned budget  circular  specifically  disallows
individual membership fees paid to private organizations.

      We also recommended that the amount of P69,800.00 be refunded  by  the
concerned employees.

We recommended  that  the  Local  Government  Unit  officials  of  the
Municipality concerned strictly observe provisions  of  Joint  Circular  No.
2015-01 of  Commission  on  Audit,  Department  of  Budget  and  Management,
Department of the Interior and Local Government, Governance  Commission  for
GOCCs and Department of National Defense dated January 8, 2015.  Failure  or
refusal to do so without justifiable cause shall  constitute  a  ground  for
administrative disciplinary action as well as disallowing the  cash  advance
granted and appropriate penal sanctions under existing laws.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
Accountant to provide and maintain a complete set of subsidiary ledgers  for
special accounts and prepare and submit financial statements as required  in
Section 313 of RA 7160 and Sections 107 and  110  of  the  NGAS  Manual  for
LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that both the Accounting and General    Services Office periodically reconcile their records to establish    the correct balances and the existence of the    Property, Plant and    Equipment accounts presented in the financial statements.  Know the    causes of the discrepancies noted and take corrective measures to prepare    necessary adjusting entries.  We further recommended that subsidiary    records for PPE be maintained by GSO to account for the receipt and    disposition of the same as well by the Accounting Office that shall    contain the details of the property, plant and equipment and livestock    accounts in the inventory control account in the general ledger.  Also, we    recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV)    to adjust public infrastructures erroneously included in the PPE    accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant updated its    subsidiary ledgers of PPE and adjusted    infrastructure assets.
observations.reason: There is an ongoing    reconciliation between    Accounting and GSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive    enjoin the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance of the    LDRRMF to the Trust Fund books and the Human Resource Management Officer    to strictly consider the qualification standards set in appointing the    DRRM Officer and staff.  Also, direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction    Officer to submit regularly the Report on Sources and Utilization of    DRRMF to the LGU’s COA Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The unspent      LDRRMF for CY    2015 transferred to Trust Fund

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant    prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to record the current portion of the    liability Loans Payable and reduce the long term obligations by the same    amount to correct amount outstanding in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal    Accountant reclassified the    account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the    official concern to coordinate with the Department of Interior and Local    Government and the AO25 Technical Working Group for the guidelines and    criteria to be used in the grant of the performance - based bonus for CY    2015.  Submit the basis for the grant of P20,000 each and cause the refund    of the amount paid to its officials and employees in excess of the    allowable amount.  Otherwise, the total amount will be disallowed in    audit.
observations.reason: The LGU submitted to the COA,    Regional Office 4 – A the appeal documents but returned for format change and for the municipality’ legal officer to prepare it.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality document    properly and completely its employment of job order workers pursuant to COA    Circular No. 2012-001.  Also, observe strictly Civil Service Commission    Memorandum Circular (CSC MC) No. 17 and 24 series of 2002 in the hiring of these workers.  We further recommended that policy guidelines be issued by the municipality on the employment of job order workers that shall be    implemented by the Office of the Human Resource and Management Office    (HRMO) of the LGU to ensure rules and regulations on merit and fitness in    public service are not circumvented.
observations.managementAction: The LGU    implemented Civil Service    Commission guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality implement    strictly provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of Philippine    Commission on Women, Department of the Interior and Local Government,    Department of Budget and Management and National Economic and Development    Authority to institutionalize gender mainstreaming mechanisms/processes in the LGU to ensure that women’s as well as men’s concerns and experiences are integral to the design, implementation, monitoring and evaluation of all legislation, policies and programs so that women and men benefit equally and inequality is not perpetuated.
observations.managementAction: The LGU requested concerned    officials for implementation of the audit    recommendation.
observations.reason: According to the incumbent    MSWD Official, GAD was not given required attention when an OIC was appointed to head the MSWD.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality observe    strictly the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003.  The Section 37 of the Act and DENR Administrative Order (DAO) No. 9, Series of 2006 issued in compliance of the said section provide for the closure of LGU existing controlled dumpsite since February 16, 2006 and put up or convert it into a sanitary landfill as an alternative.
observations.managementAction: The LGU supplemented its dumpsite with MRF equipment but will remain a controlled dump.
observations.reason: The Municipality    admitted that sanitary landfill was too costly to implement.  As of now, it has the new waste facility where excavation and soil covering of waste are now the standard practice.  In addition, the acquired MRF equipment will immediately become operational that further reduce residual waste for final disposal to the dump facility.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality duly implement the guidelines in procurement planning specified in Section 7 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.  Ensure that only goods, infrastructure projects, and consulting services which have been included in the approved APP shall be procured thereby precluding occasions for unnecessary government purchases and circumventions of the prescribed procurement procedures such as advance deliveries, ”suki system”, or unwarranted resort to alternative methods of procurement, among others.
observations.managementAction: The concerned officials of the LGU were required to attend the RA 9184 IRR to properly comply with the provisions the procurement law.
observations.reason: BAC          Secretariat  had attended seminar on RA9184 to acquire the required skill in the implementatio n of the government procurement law.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality strictly    adhere to the provisions of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA    9184.  The BAC should ensure that all procurement be done through    competitive bidding unless the law allows the use of the alternative    methods to promote transparency and competitiveness and that the most advantageous price is obtained.
observations.managementAction: The LGU committed to strictly implement rules and regulations on alternative mode procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality observe the Department of Health Administrative Order No. 163 series of 2002 in the procurement of its drugs and medicines.  Ensure that only those drugs and medicines that conform and listed in the Essential Drugs List of the Philippine National Drug Formulary (PNDF) should be requested and procured, otherwise, the requesting official should request with written justification to the National Formulary Committee for the exceptions, to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The LGU to procure only drugs listed in the DOH PNDF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the Supreme Court Decision promulgated on November 19, 2013 to revert the total amount of P234,512.44 to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund.
observations.managementAction: The LGU remitted the balance to the National Treasury

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply fully with the reporting requirements set in the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards particularly on the submission of the set of year-end financial statements in prescribed format to provide the public and stakeholders the information necessary in decision making.  Likewise, we recommended that the Municipality strictly observe deadline in the submission of accounts as prescribed in the above cited government accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the    statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned municipal official immediately settle the cash advance which remained outstanding as of December 31, 2015 and that the Municipality adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 that no additional cash advances shall be allowed unless the previous cash advance given is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made and all cash advances shall be fully liquidated at the end of the year.
observations.managementAction: The amount was paid by the LGU Official on August 5, 2016.
